Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

↔ ACP Agent (Kiro/Codex/Claude)。\\nBridge 負責 session 管理、preamble 注入、token 解析、enrichment pipeline、MCP memory。\\n主入口 `src/index.ts`（~1467 行），指令 handler 拆至 `src/commands/`，核心邏輯拆至獨立模組。\\n\\n## 模組分工\\n\\n- `index.ts` — Telegram bot 主迴圈 + per-turn enrichment pipeline + callback_query dispatcher + specialist proxy glue（~1467 行）\\n- `bot-setup.ts` — createBot factory（含 rate-limit observer）+ CommandSpec + COMMAND_SPECS + keyboard builders + registerBotCommands\\n- `run-prompt.ts` — runPrompt 核心（streaming edit、token dispatch、goal continuation、specialist proxy）\\n- `working-state.ts` — WorkingState 寫入 + delegation context 存取 + memory token helpers\\n- `session-extract.ts` — transcript 儲存、fact 萃取、handleRestart、pending-extract queue\\n- `commands/goal.ts` — /goal 狀態機指令\\n- `commands/dream.ts` — /dream 夜間維運宏\\n- `commands/schedule.ts` — /schedule 排程管理\\n- `commands/memory.ts` — /remember /memory /summary /memorytoskill + specialist-review\\n- `commands/misc.ts` — /context /usage /backup /save /wikisync /factlint 等雜項\\n- `commands/relay.ts` — relay bot handlers、relay poller init、specialist proxy、delegation context helpers\\n- `commands/deps.ts` — commands 共用依賴注入（避免循環 import）\\n- `memory.ts` — preamble 建構（buildPreambleWithBreakdown）+ fact CRUD\\n- `sessionManager.ts` — ACP session lifecycle（create/close/idle sweep）\\n- `acpClient.ts` — ACP protocol adapter（spawn agent、send prompt、stream response）\\n- `observerTransformer.ts` — 解析 agent 回覆中的 token（ASK/SEND_FILE/SCHEDULE/STICKER/...）\\n- `specialist.ts` + `specialist-create.ts` — 本機 specialist 分身（proxy/delegate/parallel）\\n- `enrichment.ts` — 每 turn 動態注入（NotebookLM routing + wiki retrieval + memory recall）\\n- `embed-router.ts` — BGE-small-zh embedding + cosine similarity（共用向量引擎）\\n- `facts-store.ts` — master log + topic Each chat owns its own Kiro ACP subprocess,\\n * so conversations stay isolated.\\n */\\nexport interface ChatSession {\\n  chatId: number;\\n  userId?: number;\\n  client: Provider;\\n  /** Which provider type is active for this session. */\\n  activeProvider: ProviderType;\\n  sessionId: string;\\n  lastUsed: number;\\n  /** Accumulated assistant text for the in-flight turn. */\\n  buffer: string;\\n  /** Accumulated thinking text for the in-flight turn (local model only). */\\n  thinkingBuffer?: string;\\n  /** Human-readable tool/status lines for the in-flight turn. */\\n  toolLog: string[];\\n  /** Rolling buffer of the agent's latest stderr output. */\\n  stderrTail: string;\\n  /** Called whenever new streamed text is appended. */\\n  onUpdate?: (session: ChatSession) => void;\\n  /** Conversation history for /save export. */\\n  history: HistoryEntry[];\\n  /** Cumulative characters sent by user (approximate context-in). */\\n  charsIn: number;\\n  /** Cumulative characters emitted by agent (approximate context-out). */\\n  charsOut: number;\\n  /** Latest usage meta reported by agent (tokens, etc.), if any. */\\n  usage?: Record<string, unknown>;\\n  /** Long-term memory preamble to inject on first prompt. */\\n  memoryPreamble: string;\\n  /**\\n   * Char-length breakdown of `memoryPreamble`. Captured once at session\\n   * create so the /context and streaming-footer UI can show where the\\n   * preamble tokens are going (tool notes / env block / memory block)\\n   * without having to re-measure the string.\\n   */\\n  preambleBreakdown: {\\n    total: number;\\n    toolNotes: number;\\n    envBlock: number;\\n    memoryBlock: number;\\n    dailylogBlock: number;\\n  };\\n  /** Set true after first prompt so we inject preamble only once. */\\n  preambleInjected: boolean;\\n  /** Stripped tail facts from preamble — used by memory-recall dedup. */\\n  tailFacts: Set<string>;\\n  /** Wiki slugs already injected this session — skip on subsequent turns. */\\n  injectedWikiSlugs: Set<string>;\\n  /** Skill names already injected this session — skip on subsequent turns. */\\n  injectedSkills: Set<string>;\\n  /**\\n   * True while a session/prompt call is in-flight against this session's\\n   * ACP client. kiro-cli rejects concurrent prompts with\\n   * `-32603 Prompt already in progress`; callers must check this flag\\n   * before calling `client.prompt()` and bail out with a friendly message\\n   * instead of racing.\\n   */\\n  inflight: boolean;\\n  /** Internal: inactivity timer handle for the current turn. */\\n  _inactivityTimer?: NodeJS.Timeout;\\n  /** Internal: true while a tool_call has started but not yet completed/failed. */\\n  _toolInFlight?: boolean;\\n  /** Internal: RELAY_DELEGATE tokens captured during streaming (workaround for CLI stripping tokens from final text). */\\n  _streamingDelegates?: string[];\\n  /** Path of the most recent user upload in this turn (consumed by RELAY_DELEGATE auto-attach). */\\n  _lastUploadPath?: string;\\n  /** Internal: meta prompts queued while session was inflight (e.g. parallel delegate results). Drained after inflight=false. */\\n  _pendingMetaQueue: Array<{ ctx: unknown; prompt: string; opts: Record<string, unknown> }>;\\n  /** Internal: consecutive tool call failures for reflexion hint injection. */\\n  _consecutiveToolFails: number;\\n}\\n\\nexport class SessionManager {\\n  private sessions = new Map<number, ChatSession>();\\n  private sweeper: NodeJS.Timeout;\\n  /** Per-chat: messages with date <= this value are silently dropped. */\\n  private _dropBefore = new Map<number, number>();\\n  /** Called before a session's agent is closed. Used for memory extraction. */\\n  public onBeforeClose?: (session: ChatSession) => Promise<void>;\\n  /** Called when the agent exits unexpectedly (crash, kill). Best-effort persistence only. */\\n  public onUnexpectedExit?: (session: ChatSession, code: number | null) => Promise<void>;\\n\\n  constructor() {\\n    this.sweeper = setInterval(() => this.sweepIdle(), 60_000);\\n    this.sweeper.unref?.();\\n  }\\n\\n  async get(chatId: number, userId?: number): Promise<ChatSession> {\\n    const existing = this.sessions.get(chatId);\\n    if (existing && !existing.client.isClosed) {\\n      existing.lastUsed = Date.now();\\n      return existing;\\n    }\\n    return this.create(chatId, userId);\\n  }\\n\\n  private async create(chatId: number, userId?: number, providerType?: ProviderType): Promise<ChatSession> {\\n    // Inject TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID so MCP children (mcp-memory) know whose\\n    // facts file to write. MEMORY_DIR is passed through too so the mcp\\n    // server doesn't need to re-read .env.\\n    const env: Record<string, string> = { MEMORY_DIR: config.memoryDir };\\n    if (userId) env.TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID = String(userId);\\n\\n    const activeType = providerType ?? config.defaultProvider;\\n    const facts = userId ? await loadFacts(userId) : \\\"\\\";\\n\\n    // Try to compress recent facts via local LLM (graceful fallback if unavailable)\\n    let compressed: string | null = null;\\n    if (facts && activeType !== \\\"local\\\") {\\n      const lines = facts.split(/\\\\r?\\\\n/).filter(l => l.trim().length > 0);\\n      const tail = config.memoryPreambleTail;\\n      if (lines.length > tail) {\\n        const { compressFactsForPreamble: compress } = await import(\\\"./local-llm.js\\\");\\n        compressed = await compress(lines.slice(-tail));\\n      }\\n    }\\n    const breakdown = activeType === \\\"local\\\"\\n      ? buildLocalPreamble(facts, userId)\\n      : buildPreambleWithBreakdown(facts, userId, { compressed });\\n\\n    // Inject working-state from previous session if available (one-shot).\\n    const wsPath = path.join(config.memoryDir, `working-state-${chatId}.md`);\\n    let workingStateBlock = \\\"\\\";\\n    try {\\n      const ws = await readFile(wsPath, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n      workingStateBlock =\\n        \\\"\\\\n[Previous session working state — use as context to resume work]\\\\n\\\" +\\n        ws +\\n        \\\"\\\\nResume from `next_action` above. Do not ask what was done last time.\\\\n\\\" +\\n        \\\"[End working state]\\\\n\\\\n\\\";\\n      await unlink(wsPath);\\n      console.log(`[session] injected working-state for chat ${chatId}`);\\n    } catch { /* file doesn't exist — normal */ }\\n\\n    const preambleText = breakdown.text + workingStateBlock;\\n    const provider = this.createProvider(activeType, env, preambleText);\\n    const session: ChatSession = {\\n      chatId,\\n      userId,\\n      client: provider,\\n      activeProvider: activeType,\\n      sessionId: \\\"\\\",\\n      lastUsed: Date.now(),\\n      buffer: \\\"\\\",\\n      toolLog: [],\\n      stderrTail: \\\"\\\",\\n      history: [],\\n      charsIn: 0,\\n      charsOut: 0,\\n      memoryPreamble: preambleText,\\n      preambleBreakdown: {\\n        total: preambleText.length,\\n        toolNotes: breakdown.toolNotes,\\n        envBlock: breakdown.envBlock,\\n        memoryBlock: breakdown.memoryBlock,\\n        dailylogBlock: breakdown.dailylogBlock,\\n      },\\n      preambleInjected: activeType !== \\\"acp\\\" ? true : false, // Direct providers use system message; ACP needs user-message injection\\n      tailFacts: breakdown.tailFacts,\\n      injectedWikiSlugs: new Set(),\\n      injectedSkills: new Set(),\\n      inflight: false,\\n      _pendingMetaQueue: [],\\n      _consecutiveToolFails: 0,\\n    };\\n\\n    provider.on(\\\"update\\\", (u: SessionUpdate) => this.applyUpdate(session, u));\\n    provider.on(\\\"stderr\\\", (chunk) => {\\n      // Keep the tail so we can surface it with error messages.\\n      session.stderrTail = (session.stderrTail + chunk).slice(-2000);\\n      // Surface only when debugging; keep quiet otherwise.\\n      if (process.env.DEBUG_ACP) {\\n        console.error(`[acp ${chatId}] ${chunk.trimEnd()}`);\\n      }\\n    });\\n    provider.on(\\\"exit\\\", (code) => {\\n      console.error(`[acp ${chatId}] agent exited (code=${code})`);\\n      // Only fire the unexpected-exit hook if this session is still registered.\\n      // Planned shutdowns (drop/sweepIdle/shutdown) remove it from the map first.\\n      const stillRegistered = this.sessions.get(chatId) === session;\\n      if (stillRegistered) {\\n        this.sessions.delete(chatId);\\n        if (this.onUnexpectedExit) {\\n          this.onUnexpectedExit(session, code).catch((e) =>\\n            console.error(`[session ${chatId}] onUnexpectedExit failed:`, e),\\n          );\\n        }\\n      }\\n    });\\n\\n    session.sessionId = await provider.initialize();\\n    this.sessions.set(chatId, session);\\n    return session;\\n  }\\n\\n  /** Factory: create the right Provider based on type. */\\n  private createProvider(type: ProviderType, env: Record<string, string>, systemPrompt?: string): Provider {\\n    if (type === \\\"acp\\\") {\\n      return new AcpProvider({\\n        command: config.agent.command,\\n        args: config.agent.args,\\n        cwd: config.sessionCwd,\\n        env,\\n        promptRetries: config.acpPromptRetries,\\n        promptRetryBaseMs: config.acpPromptRetryBaseMs,\\n        sessionCwd: config.sessionCwd,\\n      });\\n    }\\n    // All other types use DirectProvider\\n    const modelKey = type as keyof typeof config.models;\\n    const apiKeyKey = type as keyof typeof config.apiKeys;\\n    return new DirectProvider({\\n      type,\\n      model: config.models[modelKey] ?? config.models.local,\\n      baseUrl: resolveBaseUrl(type, config.ollamaUrl),\\n      apiKey: config.apiKeys[apiKeyKey] ?? \\\"\\\",\\n      systemPrompt,\\n      mcpEnv: env,\\n      // Local models have limited context — only give them memory tools.\\n      mcpServerFilter: type === \\\"local\\\" ? [\\\"memory\\\", \\\"notebooklm\\\"] : undefined,\\n    });\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Switch the active provider for a chat. Closes the old session and\\n   * creates a new one with the specified provider type.\\n   * Returns the new session.\\n   */\\n  async switchProvider(chatId: number, type: ProviderType, userId?: number): Promise<ChatSession> {\\n    const existing = this.sessions.get(chatId);\\n    if (existing) {\\n      this.sessions.delete(chatId);\\n      await existing.client.close().catch(() => {});\\n    }\\n    return this.create(chatId, userId, type);\\n  }\\n\\n  /** Start (or restart) the inactivity timer for a turn. */\\n  startInactivityTimer(session: ChatSession) {\\n    this.clearInactivityTimer(session);\\n    const ms = session._toolInFlight && config.turnToolInactivityTimeoutMs > 0\\n      ? config.turnToolInactivityTimeoutMs\\n      : config.turnInactivityTimeoutMs;\\n    if (ms <= 0) return;\\n    session._inactivityTimer = setTimeout(() => {\\n      console.warn(\\n        `[session ${session.chatId}] turn inactivity timeout (${ms}ms, toolInFlight=${!!session._toolInFlight}) — auto-cancelling`,\\n      );\\n      session.client.cancel();\\n    }, ms);\\n    session._inactivityTimer.unref?.();\\n  }\\n\\n  private resetInactivityTimer(session: ChatSession) {\\n    if (!session._inactivityTimer) return;\\n    this.startInactivityTimer(session);\\n  }\\n\\n  clearInactivityTimer(session: ChatSession) {\\n    if (session._inactivityTimer) {\\n      clearTimeout(session._inactivityTimer);\\n      session._inactivityTimer = undefined;\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  private applyUpdate(session: ChatSession, u: SessionUpdate) {\\n    this.resetInactivityTimer(session);\\n    const up = u.update;\\n    // Opportunistically capture usage-like meta the agent may attach.\\n    const meta = (up as { _meta?: Record<string, unknown> })._meta;\\n    const usage =\\n      (meta?.usage as Record<string, unknown> | undefined) ??\\n      ((up as { usage?: Record<string, unknown> }).usage);\\n    if (usage && typeof usage === \\\"object\\\") {\\n      session.usage = usage;\\n    }\\n    switch (up.sessionUpdate) {\\n      case \\\"agent_message_chunk\\\":\\n      case \\\"agent_thought_chunk\\\": {\\n        const content = up.content as { type?: string; text?: string } | undefined;\\n        if (content?.type === \\\"text\\\" && typeof content.text === \\\"string\\\") {\\n          const wasEmpty = session.buffer.length === 0 && !session.thinkingBuffer;\\n          if (up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_thought_chunk\\\") {\\n            session.thinkingBuffer = (session.thinkingBuffer || \\\"\\\") + content.text;\\n          } else {\\n            session.buffer += content.text;\\n            session.charsOut += content.text.length;\\n          }\\n          if (wasEmpty) petState(\\\"streaming\\\");\\n          // Early-capture RELAY_DELEGATE tokens during streaming.\\n          // Workaround: Kiro CLI may strip <<...>> from final text in pure-text turns,\\n          // but streaming chunks arrive unfiltered. We capture complete tokens here\\n          // so the final-render path can fall back to them if normal extraction finds nothing.\\n          const RD_RE = /<<RELAY_DELEGATE:\\\\s*([^|>\\\\r\\\\n]+?)\\\\s*\\\\|\\\\s*([^>\\\\r\\\\n]+?)\\\\s*>>/g;\\n          let m: RegExpExecArray | null;\\n          while ((m = RD_RE.exec(session.buffer)) !== null) {\\n            const token = m[0];\\n            if (!session._streamingDelegates) session._streamingDelegates = [];\\n            if (!session._streamingDelegates.includes(token)) {\\n              session._streamingDelegates.push(token);\\n            }\\n          }\\n          session.onUpdate?.(session);\\n        }\\n        break;\\n      }\\n      case \\\"tool_call\\\": {\\n        const title = (up.title as string) ?? \\\"tool\\\";\\n        session.toolLog.push(`🔧 ${title}`);\\n        session._toolInFlight = true;\\n        petState(\\\"tool\\\", title);\\n        session.onUpdate?.(session);\\n        break;\\n      }\\n      case \\\"tool_call_update\\\": {\\n        const status = up.status as string | undefined;\\n        if (status === \\\"completed\\\") {\\n          session.toolLog.push(`✅ ${(up.toolCallId as string) ?? \\\"tool\\\"}`);\\n          session._toolInFlight = false;\\n          session._consecutiveToolFails = 0;\\n          session.onUpdate?.(session);\\n        } else if (status === \\\"failed\\\") {\\n          session.toolLog.push(`❌ ${(up.toolCallId as string) ?? \\\"tool\\\"}`);\\n          session._toolInFlight = false;\\n          session._consecutiveToolFails++;\\n          if (session._consecutiveToolFails >= 2) {\\n            session.toolLog.push(\\n              `⚠️ [Reflexion hint] ${session._consecutiveToolFails} consecutive tool failures. ` +\\n              `STOP and diagnose: what assumption is wrong? Try a different approach.`\\n            );\\n          }\\n          session.onUpdate?.(session);\\n        }\\n        break;\\n      }\\n      default:\\n        // Ignore plan/user_message_chunk/etc. for the bridge.\\n        break;\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  resetTurn(session: ChatSession) {\\n    session.buffer = \\\"\\\";\\n    session.thinkingBuffer = undefined;\\n    session.toolLog = [];\\n    session.stderrTail = \\\"\\\";\\n    session._toolInFlight = false;\\n    session._streamingDelegates = undefined;\\n    this.startInactivityTimer(session);\\n  }\\n\\n  cancel(chatId: number) {\\n    const s = this.sessions.get(chatId);\\n    if (!s) return;\\n    s.client.cancel();\\n    // Force-kill escalation: if inflight doesn't clear within 60s, the CLI\\n    // is wedged. Kill the process and reset state so the user isn't stuck.\\n    if (s.inflight) {\\n      const timer = setTimeout(async () => {\\n        if (!s.inflight) return; // resolved naturally\\n        console.warn(`[cancel] chat ${chatId} still inflight after 60s — force-killing`);\\n        s.inflight = false;\\n        s._toolInFlight = false;\\n        this.clearInactivityTimer(s);\\n        await s.client.close();\\n      }, 60_000);\\n      timer.unref?.();\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  /** Enable drop-all mode: silently discard messages sent before now. */\\n  enableDropAll(chatId: number) { this._dropBefore.set(chatId, Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000)); }\\n  /** Disable drop-all mode. */\\n  disableDropAll(chatId: number) { this._dropBefore.delete(chatId); }\\n  /** Check if a message should be dropped (sent before the cutoff). */\\n  shouldDrop(chatId: number, messageDate: number): boolean {\\n    const cutoff = this._dropBefore.get(chatId);\\n    if (!cutoff) return false;\\n    if (messageDate <= cutoff) return true;\\n    // Message is newer than cutoff — auto-clear and let it through\\n    this._dropBefore.delete(chatId);\\n    return false;\\n  }\\n\\n  peek(chatId: number): ChatSession | undefined {\\n    return this.sessions.get(chatId);\\n  }\\n\\n  size(): number {\\n    return this.sessions.size;\\n  }\\n\\n  async drop(chatId: number) {\\n    const s = this.sessions.get(chatId);\\n    if (!s) return;\\n    this.sessions.delete(chatId);\\n    if (this.onBeforeClose) {\\n      try { await this.onBeforeClose(s); } catch (e) {\\n        console.error(`[session ${chatId}] onBeforeClose failed:`, e);\\n      }\\n    }\\n    await s.client.close();\\n  }\\n\\n  private async sweepIdle() {\\n    const now = Date.now();\\n    for (const [chatId, s] of this.sessions) {\\n      if (now - s.lastUsed > config.sessionIdleMs) {\\n        console.log(`[session] dropping idle chat ${chatId}`);\\n        this.sessions.delete(chatId);\\n        if (this.onBeforeClose) {\\n          try { await this.onBeforeClose(s); } catch (e) {\\n            console.error(`[session ${chatId}] onBeforeClose failed:`, e);\\n          }\\n        }\\n        s.client.close().catch(() => {});\\n      }\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  async shutdown() {\\n    clearInterval(this.sweeper);\\n    const all = Array.from(this.sessions.values());\\n    this.sessions.clear();\\n    console.log(`[session] shutdown: flushing ${all.length} session(s)`);\\n    if (this.onBeforeClose) {\\n      for (const s of all) {\\n        if (s.client.isClosed) {\\n          console.log(`[session] shutdown: skipping already-closed chat ${s.chatId}`);\\n          continue;\\n        }\\n        console.log(`[session] shutdown: running onBeforeClose for chat ${s.chatId} (history=${s.history.length})`);\\n        try { await this.onBeforeClose(s); } catch (e) {\\n          console.error(`[session ${s.chatId}] onBeforeClose failed:`, e);\\n        }\\n      }\\n    }\\n    await Promise.all(all.map((s) => s.client.isClosed ? Promise.resolve() : s.client.close()));\\n    console.log(`[session] shutdown: done`);\\n  }\\n}\\n\",\"numLines\":458,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":458}}"</outcome>

\\\"kiro-cli acp\\\");\\n  const name = (process.env.ACP_AGENT_NAME ?? \\\"\\\").trim();\\n  if (name && !base.args.includes(\\\"--agent\\\")) {\\n    base.args.push(\\\"--agent\\\", name);\\n  }\\n  return base;\\n}\\n\\nexport const config = {\\n  telegramToken: required(\\\"TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN\\\"),\\n  agent: buildAgent(),\\n  sessionCwd: path.resolve(\\n    process.env.ACP_SESSION_CWD && process.env.ACP_SESSION_CWD.trim() !== \\\"\\\"\\n      ? process.env.MEMORY_DIR\\n      : \\\"F:\\\\\\\\AI\\\\\\\\AIMemory\\\"\\n  ),\\n  // Topic classification rules file (see src/topic-rules.ts). Defaults to\\n  // <memoryDir>/topics.json so it's auto-included in /backup. Set\\n  // TOPICS_FILE to override. Default enabled.\\n  memoryPreambleTopics: (() => {\\n    const raw = (process.env.MEMORY_PREAMBLE_TOPICS ?? \\\"1\\\").trim().toLowerCase();\\n    return raw === \\\"1\\\" || raw === \\\"true\\\" || raw === \\\"yes\\\";\\n  })(),\\n  // Maximum number of topics allowed in topics.json. Enforced by the\\n  // apply_topics MCP tool when the agent proposes a new layout. Default\\n  // 50. Number(raw) : NaN;\\n    return Number.isFinite(n) && n > 0 ? Math.floor(n) : 50;\\n  })(),\\n  // Default turn budget for /goal when the user doesn't specify one.\\n  // Must be a positive integer; values >50 are clamped to 50 (hard cap\\n  // to prevent runaway loops). Set to \\\"0\\\"/\\\"false\\\" to disable.\\n  localLlmPrefilter: (() => {\\n    const raw = (process.env.LOCAL_LLM_PREFILTER ?? \\\"1\\\").trim().toLowerCase();\\n    return raw === \\\"1\\\" || raw === \\\"true\\\" || raw === \\\"yes\\\";\\n  })(),\\n  localLlmRerank: (() => {\\n    const raw = (process.env.LOCAL_LLM_RERANK ?? \\\"1\\\").trim().toLowerCase();\\n    return raw === \\\"1\\\" || raw === \\\"true\\\" || raw === \\\"yes\\\";\\n  })(),\\n  localLlmIntentFallback: (() => {\\n    const raw = (process.env.LOCAL_LLM_INTENT_FALLBACK ?? \\\"1\\\").trim().toLowerCase();\\n    return raw === \\\"1\\\" || raw === \\\"true\\\" || raw === \\\"yes\\\";\\n  })(),\\n  // RTK (Rust Token Killer) shell rewrite.   <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\src\\\\skill-router.ts\",\"content\":\"/**\\n * Skill Router — semantically match user messages to relevant skills.\\n *\\n * At init: reads SKILL.md frontmatter descriptions from SKILL_DIR, embeds each.\\n * Per turn: embeds user message, finds top matching skill(s) above threshold,\\n * returns a hint block so the agent knows which skill to prioritize.\\n */\\n\\nimport { readFileSync, readdirSync, existsSync } from \\\"node:fs\\\";\\nimport path from \\\"node:path\\\";\\nimport { embedText, cosineSim, isEmbedRouterReady } from \\\"./embed-router.js\\\";\\nimport { detectSkillDir } from \\\"./memory.js\\\";\\n\\n// ─── Config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nconst SKILL_THRESHOLD = 0.65;\\nconst SKILL_TOP_K = 2;\\nconst MIN_MSG_LENGTH = 12;\\n\\n// ─── State ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\ninterface SkillEntry {\\n  name: string;\\n  description: string;\\n  filePath: string;\\n  vector: Float32Array;\\n}\\n\\nlet skills: SkillEntry[] = [];\\nlet initialized = false;\\n\\n// ─── Public API ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport async function initSkillRouter(): Promise<void> {\\n  if (!isEmbedRouterReady()) return;\\n  const skillDir = detectSkillDir();\\n  if (!existsSync(skillDir)) return;\\n\\n  skills = [];\\n  let entries: string[];\\n  try {\\n    entries = readdirSync(skillDir);\\n  } catch { return; }\\n\\n  for (const name of entries) {\\n    const skillFile = path.join(skillDir, name, \\\"SKILL.md\\\");\\n    if (!existsSync(skillFile)) continue;\\n    const desc = extractDescription(skillFile);\\n    if (!desc) continue;\\n    const vec = await embedText(desc);\\n    if (!vec) continue;\\n    skills.push({ name, description: desc, filePath: skillFile, vector: vec });\\n  }\\n  initialized = true;\\n  console.log(`[skill-router] ready: ${skills.length} skill vectors`);\\n}\\n\\nexport function isSkillRouterReady(): boolean {\\n  return initialized && skills.length > 0;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface SkillHit {\\n  name: string;\\n  description: string;\\n  score: number;\\n}\\n\\nexport async function routeToSkills(userMsg: string): Promise<SkillHit[]> {\\n  if (!initialized || skills.length === 0) return [];\\n  if (userMsg.trim().length < MIN_MSG_LENGTH) return [];\\n\\n  const queryVec = await embedText(userMsg);\\n  if (!queryVec) return [];\\n\\n  const scored: SkillHit[] = [];\\n  for (const skill of skills) {\\n    const score = cosineSim(queryVec, skill.vector);\\n    if (score >= SKILL_THRESHOLD) {\\n      scored.push({ name: skill.name, description: skill.description, score });\\n    }\\n  }\\n  if (scored.length === 0) return [];\\n  scored.sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score);\\n  return scored.slice(0, SKILL_TOP_K);\\n}\\n\\nexport function formatSkillHint(hits: SkillHit[]): string {\\n  if (hits.length === 0) return \\\"\\\";\\n  const lines = hits.map(\\n    (h) => `- ${h.name} (score ${h.score.toFixed(2)}): ${h.description.slice(0, 100)}`\\n  );\\n  return (\\n    `[Skill routing — these skills are most relevant to this message, follow their instructions]\\\\n` +\\n    lines.join(\\\"\\\\n\\\") +\\n    `\\\\n[End skill routing]\\\\n\\\\n`\\n  );\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Internals ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nfunction extractDescription(filePath: string): string | null {\\n  try {\\n    const raw = readFileSync(filePath, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n    // Parse YAML frontmatter: between first --- and second ---\\n    const match = raw.match(/^---\\\\s*\\\\n([\\\\s\\\\S]*?)\\\\n---/);\\n    if (!match) return null;\\n    const fm = match[1];\\n    // Extract description field\\n    const descMatch = fm.match(/^description:\\\\s*(.+)$/m);\\n    if (!descMatch) return null;\\n    return descMatch[1].trim();\\n  } catch {\\n    return null;\\n  }\\n}\\n\",\"numLines\":116,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":116}}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

recent sessions for recurring user message patterns that have no\\n * matching skill, producing a candidate list for /dream memorytoskill.\\n */\\nimport { readdirSync, readFileSync, existsSync, statSync } from \\\"node:fs\\\";\\nimport path from \\\"node:path\\\";\\nimport { config } from \\\"./config.js\\\";\\nimport { detectSkillDir } from \\\"./memory.js\\\";\\n\\nexport interface SkillCandidate {\\n  pattern: string;          // representative keyword/phrase\\n  count: number;            // how many sessions it appeared in\\n  examples: string[];       // up to 3 representative user messages\\n}\\n\\nconst DAYS_WINDOW = 7;\\nconst MIN_SESSIONS = 3;    // must appear in ≥3 different sessions\\nconst STOP_WORDS = new Set([\\n  \\\"的\\\", \\\"了\\\", \\\"是\\\", \\\"在\\\", \\\"我\\\", \\\"有\\\", \\\"和\\\", \\\"就\\\", \\\"不\\\", \\\"人\\\", \\\"都\\\", \\\"一\\\", \\\"這\\\",\\n  \\\"那\\\", \\\"要\\\", \\\"會\\\", \\\"對\\\", \\\"也\\\", \\\"說\\\", \\\"到\\\", \\\"可以\\\", \\\"沒有\\\", \\\"什麼\\\", \\\"怎麼\\\",\\n  \\\"the\\\", \\\"a\\\", \\\"an\\\", \\\"is\\\", \\\"are\\\", \\\"was\\\", \\\"were\\\", \\\"be\\\", \\\"been\\\", \\\"being\\\",\\n  \\\"have\\\", \\\"has\\\", \\\"had\\\", \\\"do\\\", \\\"does\\\", \\\"did\\\", \\\"will\\\", \\\"would\\\", \\\"could\\\",\\n  \\\"should\\\", \\\"may\\\", \\\"might\\\", \\\"shall\\\", \\\"can\\\", \\\"need\\\", \\\"dare\\\", \\\"ought\\\",\\n  \\\"to\\\", \\\"of\\\", \\\"in\\\", \\\"for\\\", \\\"on\\\", \\\"with\\\", \\\"at\\\", \\\"by\\\", \\\"from\\\", \\\"as\\\",\\n  \\\"into\\\", \\\"through\\\", \\\"during\\\", \\\"before\\\", \\\"after\\\", \\\"above\\\", \\\"below\\\",\\n  \\\"it\\\", \\\"this\\\", \\\"that\\\", \\\"these\\\", \\\"those\\\", \\\"i\\\", \\\"you\\\", \\\"he\\\", \\\"she\\\",\\n  \\\"we\\\", \\\"they\\\", \\\"me\\\", \\\"him\\\", \\\"her\\\", \\\"us\\\", \\\"them\\\", \\\"my\\\", \\\"your\\\",\\n  \\\"his\\\", \\\"its\\\", \\\"our\\\", \\\"their\\\", \\\"and\\\", \\\"but\\\", \\\"or\\\", \\\"not\\\", \\\"no\\\",\\n  \\\"好\\\", \\\"嗯\\\", \\\"ok\\\", \\\"yes\\\", \\\"no\\\", \\\"push\\\", \\\"繼續\\\", \\\"done\\\",\\n]);\\n\\n/** Extract user messages from a session markdown file. */\\nfunction extractUserMessages(content: string): string[] {\\n  const msgs: string[] = [];\\n  const blocks = content.split(/^### 👤 User/m);\\n  for (let i = 1; i < blocks.length; i++) {\\n    // Take text until next ### header\\n    const end = blocks[i].indexOf(\\\"\\\\n### \\\");\\n    const raw = end > 0 ? blocks[i].slice(0, end) : blocks[i];\\n    // Strip metadata lines (Intent hint, Skill routing, timestamps, relay captions)\\n    const lines = raw.split(\\\"\\\\n\\\").filter(l =>\\n      !l.startsWith(\\\"[\\\") && !l.startsWith(\\\"---\\\") && !l.startsWith(\\\"### \\\") &&\\n      !/^\\\\s*\\\\(.*[上下]午\\\\d/.test(l) &&\\n      !/^\\\\s*這是委派/.test(l) &&\\n      l.trim().length > 2\\n    );\\n    const msg = lines.join(\\\" \\\").trim();\\n    if (msg.length > 5 && msg.length < 200) msgs.push(msg);\\n  }\\n  return msgs;\\n}\\n\\n/** Tokenize a message into meaningful bigrams. */\\nfunction extractBigrams(msg: string): string[] {\\n  const words = msg.toLowerCase()\\n    .replace(/[^\\\\w\\\\u4e00-\\\\u9fff\\\\s]/g, \\\" \\\")\\n    .split(/\\\\s+/)\\n    .filter(w => w.length > 1 && !STOP_WORDS.has(w));\\n  const bigrams: string[] = [];\\n  for (let i = 0; i < words.length - 1; i++) {\\n    bigrams.push(`${words[i]} ${words[i + 1]}`);\\n  }\\n  // Also include single meaningful words ≥3 chars\\n  for (const w of words) {\\n    if (w.length >= 3) bigrams.push(w);\\n  }\\n  return bigrams;\\n}\\n\\n/** Load existing skill keywords for comparison. */\\nfunction loadSkillKeywords(): Set<string> {\\n  const skillDir = detectSkillDir();\\n  const keywords = new Set<string>();\\n  if (!existsSync(skillDir)) return keywords;\\n  for (const entry of readdirSync(skillDir, { withFileTypes: true })) {\\n    if (!entry.isDirectory()) continue;\\n    // Add the skill folder name as a keyword\\n    keywords.add(entry.name.replace(/^ms-/, \\\"\\\").replace(/-/g, \\\" \\\"));\\n    // Read SKILL.md description for additional keywords\\n    const skillFile = path.join(skillDir, entry.name, \\\"SKILL.md\\\");\\n    if (!existsSync(skillFile)) continue;\\n    const content = readFileSync(skillFile, \\\"utf8\\\").slice(0, 500);\\n    const descMatch = content.match(/description:\\\\s*(.+)/);\\n    if (descMatch) {\\n      const desc = descMatch[1].toLowerCase();\\n      // Extract Chinese/English words from description\\n      const descWords = desc.replace(/[^\\\\w\\\\u4e00-\\\\u9fff\\\\s]/g, \\\" \\\").split(/\\\\s+/)\\n        .filter(w => w.length >= 3);\\n      for (const w of descWords) keywords.add(w);\\n    }\\n  }\\n  return keywords;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Scan recent sessions and produce skill candidates.\\n * Returns patterns that appear in ≥3 sessions with no matching skill.\\n */\\nexport function suggestSkills(): SkillCandidate[] {\\n  const sessDir = path.join(config.memoryDir, \\\"sessions\\\");\\n  if (!existsSync(sessDir)) return [];\\n\\n  const cutoff = Date.now() - DAYS_WINDOW * 86400_000;\\n  const files = readdirSync(sessDir)\\n    .filter(f => f.startsWith(\\\"session-\\\") && f.endsWith(\\\".md\\\"))\\n    .filter(f => {\\n      const full = path.join(sessDir, f);\\n      try {\\n        return statSync(full).mtimeMs >= cutoff;\\n      } catch { return false; }\\n    });\\n\\n  // Count bigrams across sessions (deduplicated per session)\\n  const bigramSessions = new Map<string, Set<string>>(); // bigram → set of session filenames\\n  const bigramExamples = new Map<string, string[]>();     // bigram → example messages\\n\\n  for (const file of files) {\\n    const content = readFileSync(path.join(sessDir, file), \\\"utf8\\\");\\n    const msgs = extractUserMessages(content);\\n    const sessionBigrams = new Set<string>();\\n    for (const msg of msgs) {\\n      for (const bg of extractBigrams(msg)) {\\n        if (!sessionBigrams.has(bg)) {\\n          sessionBigrams.add(bg);\\n          if (!bigramSessions.has(bg)) bigramSessions.set(bg, new Set());\\n          bigramSessions.get(bg)!.add(file);\\n          if (!bigramExamples.has(bg)) bigramExamples.set(bg, []);\\n          const ex = bigramExamples.get(bg)!;\\n          if (ex.length < 3) ex.push(msg.slice(0, 100));\\n        }\\n      }\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  // Filter: ≥MIN_SESSIONS occurrences\\n  const skillKeywords = loadSkillKeywords();\\n  const candidates: SkillCandidate[] = [];\\n\\n  for (const [bigram, sessions] of bigramSessions) {\\n    if (sessions.size < MIN_SESSIONS) continue;\\n    // Skip if matches an existing skill keyword\\n    if (skillKeywords.has(bigram)) continue;\\n    // Skip if any word in the bigram is a skill keyword\\n    const words = bigram.split(\\\" \\\");\\n    if (words.some(w => skillKeywords.has(w))) continue;\\n\\n    candidates.push({\\n      pattern: bigram,\\n      count: sessions.size,\\n      examples: bigramExamples.get(bigram) ?? [],\\n    });\\n  }\\n\\n  // Sort by frequency descending, take top 10\\n  candidates.sort((a, b) => b.count - a.count);\\n  return candidates.slice(0, 10);\\n}\\n\",\"numLines\":159,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":159}}"</outcome>

  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\src\\\\skill-router.ts\",\"content\":\"/**\\n * Skill Router — semantically match user messages to relevant skills.\\n *\\n * At init: reads SKILL.md frontmatter descriptions from SKILL_DIR, embeds each.\\n * Per turn: embeds user message, finds top matching skill(s) above threshold,\\n * returns a hint block so the agent knows which skill to prioritize.\\n */\\n\\nimport { readFileSync, readdirSync, existsSync } from \\\"node:fs\\\";\\nimport path from \\\"node:path\\\";\\nimport { embedText, cosineSim, isEmbedRouterReady } from \\\"./embed-router.js\\\";\\nimport { detectSkillDir } from \\\"./memory.js\\\";\\n\\n// ─── Config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nconst SKILL_THRESHOLD = 0.65;\\nconst SKILL_TOP_K = 2;\\nconst MIN_MSG_LENGTH = 12;\\n\\n// ─── State ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\ninterface SkillEntry {\\n  name: string;\\n  description: string;\\n  filePath: string;\\n  vector: Float32Array;\\n}\\n\\nlet skills: SkillEntry[] = [];\\nlet initialized = false;\\n\\n// ─── Public API ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport async function initSkillRouter(): Promise<void> {\\n  if (!isEmbedRouterReady()) return;\\n  const skillDir = detectSkillDir();\\n  if (!existsSync(skillDir)) return;\\n\\n  skills = [];\\n  let entries: string[];\\n  try {\\n    entries = readdirSync(skillDir);\\n  } catch { return; }\\n\\n  for (const name of entries) {\\n    const skillFile = path.join(skillDir, name, \\\"SKILL.md\\\");\\n    if (!existsSync(skillFile)) continue;\\n    const desc = extractDescription(skillFile);\\n    if (!desc) continue;\\n    const vec = await embedText(desc);\\n    if (!vec) continue;\\n    skills.push({ name, description: desc, filePath: skillFile, vector: vec });\\n  }\\n  initialized = true;\\n  console.log(`[skill-router] ready: ${skills.length} skill vectors`);\\n}\\n\\nexport function isSkillRouterReady(): boolean {\\n  return initialized && skills.length > 0;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface SkillHit {\\n  name: string;\\n  description: string;\\n  score: number;\\n}\\n\\nexport async function routeToSkills(userMsg: string): scored.push({ name: skill.name, description: skill.description, score });\\n    }\\n  }\\n  if (scored.length === 0) return [];\\n  scored.sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score);\\n  return scored.slice(0, lines.join(\\\"\\\\n\\\") +\\n    `\\\\n[End skill routing]\\\\n\\\\n`\\n  );\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Internals ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nfunction Runs as a child of the ACP agent\\n// (spawned via ~/.kiro/agents/main.json mcpServers, or\\n// ~/.codex/config.toml [mcp_servers.memory] for Codex CLI standalone).\\n// Exposes remember / list_facts / forget / apply_topics / propose_topics.\\n//\\n// The active user id is taken from one of these, in priority order:\\n//   1. env TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID  — injected by telegram-kiro bridge\\n//   2. env MEMORY_USER_ID     — static config for standalone agents\\n//   3. file <MEMORY_DIR>/.user-id  — single-line plain text\\n//\\n// MEMORY_DIR env overrides the facts directory (defaults to\\n// F:\\\\AI\\\\AIMemory on Kiro, set to F:\\\\AI_Codex\\\\AIMemory on Codex box).\\nimport { mkdir, readFile, appendFile } from \\\"node:fs/promises\\\";\\nimport { readFileSync } from \\\"node:fs\\\";\\nimport path from \\\"node:path\\\";\\nimport { Server } from \\\"@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/index.js\\\";\\nimport { StdioServerTransport } from \\\"@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/stdio.js\\\";\\nimport {\\n  CallToolRequestSchema,\\n  ListToolsRequestSchema,\\n} from \\\"@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/types.js\\\";\\nimport { keywordsForTopic, classifyFact, classifyFactAsync } from \\\"./topic-rules.js\\\";\\nimport {\\n  appendFactShardOnly,\\n  readTopicFacts,\\n  formatFactLine,\\n  forgetMatch,\\n  forgetCommit,\\n  readAllFactsForReview,\\n  applyTopics,\\n  computeTopicsToken,\\n  normaliseProposedTopics,\\n  resolveMaxTopics,\\n} from \\\"./facts-store.js\\\";\\nimport { factExists, insertFact, deleteFact } from \\\"./memory-db.js\\\";\\nimport { ingestPrepare, ingestSave, listIndexes, queryIndex, findStaleIndexes, ingestUpdate, buildCrossRefs } from \\\"./page-index.js\\\";\\n\\nconst MEMORY_DIR = path.resolve(\\n  process.env.MEMORY_DIR && process.env.MEMORY_DIR.trim() !== \\\"\\\"\\n    ? process.env.MEMORY_DIR\\n    : \\\"F:\\\\\\\\AI\\\\\\\\AIMemory\\\"\\n);\\n\\nfunction activeUserId(): number {\\n  // Primary: TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID — set automatically by the telegram-kiro\\n  // bridge when it spawns the agent. Accept the shorter alias\\n  // MEMORY_USER_ID for standalone use (e.g. Codex CLI where the bridge\\n  // doesn't inject env, and the user statically configures the MCP\\n  // server via ~/.codex/config.toml [mcp_servers.memory.env]).\\n  // Finally, fall back to reading <MEMORY_DIR>/.user-id so the user can\\n  // drop a single-line file there instead of editing TOML.\\n  const envRaw =\\n    (process.env.TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID ?? \\\"\\\").trim() ||\\n    (process.env.MEMORY_USER_ID ?? \\\"\\\").trim();\\n  if (envRaw) {\\n    const n = Number(envRaw);\\n    if (Number.isFinite(n) && n > 0) return n;\\n  }\\n  try {\\n    const filePath = path.join(MEMORY_DIR, \\\".user-id\\\");\\n    const raw = readFileSync(filePath, \\\"utf8\\\").trim();\\n    const n = Number(raw);\\n    if (Number.isFinite(n) && n > 0) return n;\\n  } catch {\\n    // ignore — fall through to error\\n  }\\n  throw new Error(\\n    \\\"memory MCP: active user id not configured. Set one of:\\\\n\\\" +\\n      \\\"  - env TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID (how the telegram-kiro bridge normally injects it), or\\\\n\\\" +\\n      \\\"  - env MEMORY_USER_ID (set in ~/.codex/config.toml [mcp_servers.memory.env] for standalone Codex use), or\\\\n\\\" +\\n      `  - a plain-text file ${path.join(MEMORY_DIR, \\\".user-id\\\")} containing just the Telegram user id.`\\n  );\\n}\\n\\nfunction factsPath(userId: number): string {\\n  return path.join(MEMORY_DIR, `facts-${userId}.md`);\\n}\\n\\nasync function remember(text: string, topic?: string): Promise<string> {\\n  const line = text.trim();\\n  if (!line) throw new Error(\\\"text is empty\\\");\\n  const userId = activeUserId();\\n  await mkdir(MEMORY_DIR, { recursive: true });\\n\\n  // Dedup check: precise content hash (SQLite) then Jaccard fallback.\\n  const targetTopic = topic?.trim() || (await classifyFactAsync(line)) || \\\"misc\\\";\\n  if (factExists(line)) {\\n    return `⚠️ Exact duplicate detected (content hash match). Not appended.`;\\n  }\\n  const existingFacts = readTopicFacts(userId, targetTopic);\\n  const dupWarning = checkDuplicate(line, existingFacts);\\n\\n  const formatted = formatFactLine(line);\\n  await appendFile(factsPath(userId), formatted, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n  // Mirror to topic shard. If caller supplied an explicit topic we trust\\n  // it; otherwise classifyFact infers from the text.\\n  const shard = appendFactShardOnly({\\n    userId,\\n    formattedLine: formatted,\\n    rawText: line,\\n    topic: topic?.trim() || undefined,\\n  });\\n  // Mirror to SQLite index\\n  const createdAt = new Date().toISOString();\\n  insertFact(line, targetTopic, createdAt);\\n  const shardNote = shard ? `, shard=${path.basename(shard.file)}` : \\\"\\\";\\n  const warn = dupWarning ? `\\\\n⚠️ Possible duplicate in shard \\\"${targetTopic}\\\": \\\"${dupWarning}\\\"` : \\\"\\\";\\n  return `ok: appended to ${factsPath(userId)}${shardNote}${warn}`;\\n}\\n\\nfunction stripFactPrefix(s: string): string {\\n  return s.replace(/^\\\\s*-\\\\s*(?:\\\\[f_[0-9a-f]{6}\\\\]\\\\s*)?\\\\[[^\\\\]]+\\\\]\\\\s*/, \\\"\\\").trim();\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Check if a new fact is semantically similar to existing facts using\\n * Jaccard word-set similarity. Returns the most similar existing fact\\n * text if similarity > 0.7, or null if no duplicate found.\\n */\\nfunction checkDuplicate(newFact: string, existingFacts: string[]): string | null {\\n  if (existingFacts.length === 0) return null;\\n  const newWords = wordSet(newFact);\\n  if (newWords.size < 3) return null; // too short to meaningfully compare\\n  let bestScore = 0;\\n  let bestFact = \\\"\\\";\\n  for (const existing of existingFacts) {\\n    const existWords = wordSet(existing);\\n    const intersection = new Set([...newWords].filter((w) => existWords.has(w)));\\n    const union = new Set([...newWords, ...existWords]);\\n    const jaccard = union.size > 0 ? intersection.size / union.size : 0;\\n    if (jaccard > bestScore) {\\n      bestScore = jaccard;\\n      bestFact = existing;\\n    }\\n  }\\n  return bestScore >= 0.7 ? bestFact : null;\\n}\\n\\nfunction wordSet(text: string): Set<string> {\\n  return new Set(\\n    text.toLowerCase().replace(/[^\\\\w\\\\u4e00-\\\\u9fff]+/g, \\\" \\\").trim().split(/\\\\s+/).filter(Boolean)\\n  );\",\"numLines\":150,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":757}}"</outcome>

Convenience so callers don't re-measure. */\\n  total: number;\\n  /** sendFileNote + askNote + scheduleNote combined. */\\n  toolNotes: number;\\n  /** Environment block (MEMORY_DIR / SKILL_DIR / AGENT_CONFIG_DIR / USER_ID). */\\n  envBlock: number;\\n  /** Long-term memory block (recent facts + topic index). 0 if no facts. */\\n  memoryBlock: number;\\n  /** Daily log block (most recent dailylog). 0 if none. */\\n  dailylogBlock: number;\\n  /** Wiki index block (page titles from wiki/index.md). 0 if no wiki. */\\n  wikiBlock: number;\\n  /** Document tree indexes listing. 0 if no indexes. */\\n  indexBlock: number;\\n  /** Available agent-developed scripts (tool-* in scripts/). 0 if none. */\\n  scriptsBlock: number;\\n  /** Stripped tail facts included in preamble — for dedup in memory-recall. */\\n  tailFacts: Set<string>;\\n}\\n\\n\\n\\n/**\\n * Like buildPreamble but also returns per-section char lengths so the\\n * bridge can display where context tokens are going. { type Database as DatabaseType } from \\\"better-sqlite3\\\";\\nimport { createHash } from \\\"node:crypto\\\";\\nimport { readFileSync, existsSync } from \\\"node:fs\\\";\\nimport path from Set(parsed.map((f) => f.hash));\\n\\n  // Delete facts no longer in master\\n  const toDelete = [...existingHashes].filter((h) => !newHashes.has(h));\\n  if (toDelete.length > 0) {\\n    const del =  );\\n    const insFts = db.prepare(\\n      \\\"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO facts_fts (content_hash, text) VALUES (?, ?)\\\",\\n    );\\n    const tx = db.transaction(() => {\\n      for (const f of */\\nexport function deleteFact(text: string): void {\\n  if (!db) return;\\n  const hash = contentHash(text);\\n  db.prepare(\\\"DELETE FROM facts WHERE content_hash = ?\\\").run(hash);\\n  db.prepare(\\\"DELETE FROM facts_fts WHERE content_hash = ?\\\").run(hash);\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Check if a fact with the same content hash already exists.\\n */\\nexport function factExists(text: DEFAULT_TOP_K;\\n  const threshold = opts.threshold ?? DEFAULT_THRESHOLD;\\n  const enableDecay = opts.enableDecay ?? true;\\n\\n  const queryVec = await getEmbeddingCached(query);\\n\\n  // 1. FTS5 BM25 results\\n  const bm25Hits = fts5Search(query);\\n\\n  // 2. Vector cosine results (brute-force over all facts with cached embeddings)\\n  const vectorHits = queryVec ? await vectorSearch(queryVec) : [];\\n\\n  // 3. Merge scores\\n  const scoreMap = new Map<string, { text: string; topic: string; createdAt: string; bm25: number; vector: number }>();\\n\\n  for (const h of bm25Hits) {\\n    scoreMap.set(h.hash, { text: h.text, topic: h.topic, createdAt: h.createdAt, bm25: h.score, vector: 0 });\\n  }\\n  for (const h of vectorHits) {\\n    const existing = scoreMap.get(h.hash);\\n    if (existing) {\\n      existing.vector = h.score;\\n    } else {\\n      scoreMap.set(h.hash, { text: h.text, topic: h.topic, createdAt: h.createdAt, bm25: 0, vector: h.score });\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  // 4. Compute final score with decay\\n  const now = Date.now();\\n  const results: HybridHit[] = [];\\n\\n  for (const [, entry] of scoreMap) {\\n    if (opts.excludeTexts?.has(entry.text)) continue;\\n\\n    let score = VECTOR_WEIGHT * entry.vector + BM25_WEIGHT * entry.bm25;\\n\\n    // Source-tier boost: curated topics rank higher\\n    const tierBoost = SOURCE_TIER_BOOST[entry.topic] ?? DEFAULT_TIER_BOOST;\\n    score *= tierBoost;\\n\\n    // Temporal decay\\n    if (enableDecay && entry.createdAt) {\\n      const ageDays = (now - new Date(entry.createdAt).getTime()) / 86_400_000;\\n      if (ageDays > 0) {\\n        score *= Math.exp(-LAMBDA * ageDays);\\n      }\\n    }\\n\\n    if (score >= threshold) {\\n      results.push({ text: entry.text, topic: entry.topic, score, createdAt: entry.createdAt });\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  results.sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score);\\n  return results.slice(0, topK);\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Internals ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nfunction createSchema(database: DatabaseType): void {\\n  database.exec(`\\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS facts (\\n      content_hash TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n      text TEXT NOT NULL,\\n      topic TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'misc',\\n      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''\\n    );\\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS embedding_cache (\\n      content_hash TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n      vector BLOB NOT NULL\\n    );\\n    CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS facts_fts USING fts5(\\n      content_hash UNINDEXED,\\n      text,\\n      tokenize='unicode61'\\n    );\\n  `);\\n}\\n\\nexport function contentHash(text: string): string {\\n  return createHash(\\\"sha256\\\").update(text.trim()).digest(\\\"hex\\\").slice(0, 16);\\n}\\n\\ninterface ParsedFact {\\n  text: string;\\n  hash: string;\\n  topic: string;\\n  createdAt: string;\\n}\\n\\nfunction parseMasterLog(filePath: string): ParsedFact[] {\\n  if (!existsSync(filePath)) return [];\\n  const raw = readFileSync(filePath, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n  const results: ParsedFact[] = [];\\n  for (const line of raw.split(/\\\\r?\\\\n/)) {\\n    // Format: \\\"- [2026-05-16T10:00:00.000Z] fact text here\\\"\\n    const match = line.match(/^\\\\s*-\\\\s*\\\\[([^\\\\]]+)\\\\]\\\\s*(.+)$/);\\n    if (!match) continue;\\n    const createdAt = match[1];\\n    const text = match[2].trim();\\n    if (!text) continue;\\n    const topic = classifyFact(text) || \\\"misc\\\";\\n    results.push({ text, hash: contentHash(text), topic, createdAt });\\n  }\\n  return results;\\n}\\n\\ninterface FtsHit {\\n  hash: string;\\n  text: string;\\n  topic: string;\\n  createdAt: string;\\n  score: number;\\n}\\n\\nfunction fts5Search(query: string): FtsHit[] {\\n maxScore }));\\n  } catch {\\n    // FTS5 match can throw on malformed queries\\n    return [];\\n  }\\n}\\n\\ninterface VectorHit {\\n  hash: string;\\n  text: string;\\n  topic: string;\\n  createdAt: string;\\n  score: number;\\n}\\n\\nasync function vectorSearch(queryVec: Float32Array): Promise<VectorHit[]> {\\n  if (!db) return [];\\n  // Get all facts that have cached embeddings\\n  const rows = db\\n    .prepare(\\n      `SELECT f.content_hash as hash, f.text, f.topic, f.created_at as createdAt, ec.vector\\n       FROM facts f\\n       JOIN embedding_cache ec ON ec.content_hash = f.content_hash`,\\n    )\\n    .all() as Array<{ hash: string; text: string; topic: string; createdAt: string; vector: Buffer }>;\\n\\n  const hits: VectorHit[] = [];\\n  for (const row of rows) <observed_from_primary_session>
  <what_happened>Read</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-06-22T09:25:10.463Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>G:\AI\telegram-kiro-bridge-main</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"file_path\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\src\\\\memory-recall.ts\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\src\\\\memory-recall.ts\",\"content\":\"/**\\n * Memory Recall — dynamically inject relevant topic shard facts into context\\n * based on semantic similarity between the user message and topic representations.\\n *\\n * At init: builds a vector for each topic from \\\"topic-name: keyword1, keyword2, ...\\\"\\n * Per turn: embeds user message, finds top matching topics above threshold,\\n * reads those shards and returns facts to inject.\\n */\\n\\nimport { embedText, cosineSim, isEmbedRouterReady } from \\\"./embed-router.js\\\";\\nimport { readTopicFacts, listTopicShards } from \\\"./facts-store.js\\\";\\nimport { getTopicRules } from \\\"./topic-rules.js\\\";\\nimport { hybridSearch, getDb, type HybridHit } from \\\"./memory-db.js\\\";\\nimport { rerankRecallHits } from \\\"./local-llm.js\\\";\\nimport { config } from \\\"./config.js\\\";\\n\\n// ─── Config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/** Minimum cosine similarity to consider a topic relevant. */\\nconst RECALL_THRESHOLD = 0.65;\\n/** Max topics to recall per turn. */\\nconst RECALL_TOP_K = 3;\\n/** Max total facts to inject (avoid blowing up context). */\\nconst RECALL_MAX_FACTS = 30;\\n/** Skip messages shorter than this (too short for meaningful embedding). */\\nconst MIN_MSG_LENGTH = 10;\\n\\n// ─── State ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nlet topicVectors: Map<string, Float32Array> = new Map(); // topic name → vector\\nlet initialized = false;\\n\\n// ─── Public API ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/**\\n * Build topic vectors from current topics.json rules.\\n * Call after initEmbedRouter() so the model is loaded.\\n */\\nexport async function initMemoryRecall(): Promise<void> {\\n  if (!isEmbedRouterReady()) return;\\n  const rules = getTopicRules();\\n  topicVectors.clear();\\n  for (const rule of rules) {\\n    if (rule.topic === \\\"misc\\\") continue; // skip catchall\\n    // Build repr: \\\"topic-name: keyword1, keyword2, keyword3\\\"\\n    const repr = `${rule.topic}: ${rule.keywords.join(\\\", \\\")}`;\\n    const vec = await embedText(repr);\\n    if (vec) topicVectors.set(rule.topic, vec);\\n  }\\n  initialized = true;\\n  console.log(`[memory-recall] ready: ${topicVectors.size} topic vectors`);\\n}\\n\\nexport function isMemoryRecallReady(): boolean {\\n  return initialized && topicVectors.size > 0;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface RecallHit {\\n  topic: string;\\n  score: number;\\n  facts: string[]; // stripped fact text\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Given a user message, find semantically relevant topic shards and return\\n * their facts. Returns empty array if nothing matches or module not ready.\\n * `recentFacts` (optional) is the set of facts already in the preamble tail —\\n * these are excluded from recall results to avoid duplication.\\n * `excludeTopics` (optional) skips topics already covered by wiki retrieval.\\n */\\nexport async function recallRelevantFacts(\\n  userMsg: string,\\n  userId: number | string,\\n  recentFacts?: Set<string>,\\n  excludeTopics?: Set<string>,\\n  /** If provided, only consider these topics (scope filter for specialists). */\\n  includeTopics?: Set<string>,\\n  /** Override threshold (e.g. lower when scope already narrows candidates). */\\n  threshold = RECALL_THRESHOLD,\\n): Promise<RecallHit[]> {\\n  if (!initialized || topicVectors.size === 0) return [];\\n  if (userMsg.trim().length < MIN_MSG_LENGTH) return [];\\n\\n  // ─── Hybrid path: use SQLite FTS5 + vector when DB is available ────────\\n  if (getDb()) {\\n    const hits = await hybridSearch(userMsg, {\\n      topK: RECALL_MAX_FACTS,\\n      threshold: 0.2, // lower threshold since decay reduces scores\\n      enableDecay: true,\\n      excludeTexts: recentFacts,\\n    });\\n    if (hits.length === 0) return [];\\n\\n    // Rerank with local LLM if we have more candidates than needed\\n    let rankedHits = hits;\\n    if (config.localLlmRerank && hits.length > RECALL_MAX_FACTS / 2) {\\n      try {\\n        const t0 = Date.now();\\n        const reranked = await rerankRecallHits(\\n          userMsg,\\n          hits.map(h => ({ text: h.text, topic: h.topic || \\\"misc\\\", score: h.score })),\\n          RECALL_MAX_FACTS / 2,\\n        );\\n        console.log(`[memory-recall] rerank ${hits.length}→${reranked.length} (${Date.now() - t0}ms)`);\\n        if (reranked.length > 0) {\\n          rankedHits = reranked.map(r => ({\\n            text: r.text,\\n            topic: r.topic,\\n            score: r.rerankScore,\\n          })) as typeof hits;\\n        }\\n      } catch { /* rerank failed — use original order */ }\\n    }\\n\\n    // Group by topic for output format compatibility\\n    const byTopic = new Map<string, { score: number; facts: string[] }>();\\n    for (const h of rankedHits) {\\n      const topic = h.topic || \\\"misc\\\";\\n      if (excludeTopics?.has(topic)) continue;\\n      if (includeTopics && !includeTopics.has(topic)) continue;\\n      const entry = byTopic.get(topic) ?? { score: 0, facts: [] };\\n      entry.score = Math.max(entry.score, h.score);\\n      entry.facts.push(h.text);\\n      byTopic.set(topic, entry);\\n    }\\n\\n    const results: RecallHit[] = [];\\n    for (const [topic, { score, facts }] of byTopic) {\\n      results.push({ topic, score, facts });\\n    }\\n    results.sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score);\\n    return results.slice(0, RECALL_TOP_K);\\n  }\\n\\n  // ─── Fallback: original topic-vector approach ──────────────────────────\\n\\n  const queryVec = await embedText(userMsg);\\n  if (!queryVec) return [];\\n\\n  // Score all topics\\n  const scored: Array<{ topic: string; score: number }> = [];\\n  for (const [topic, vec] of topicVectors) {\\n    if (excludeTopics?.has(topic)) continue;\\n    if (includeTopics && !includeTopics.has(topic)) continue;\\n    const score = cosineSim(queryVec, vec);\\n    if (score >= threshold) {\\n      scored.push({ topic, score });\\n    }\\n  }\\n  if (scored.length === 0) return [];\\n  scored.sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score);\\n\\n  // Take top-K topics\\n  const topHits = scored.slice(0, RECALL_TOP_K);\\n\\n  // Read shard facts for each hit, respecting total cap\\n  const results: RecallHit[] = [];\\n  let totalFacts = 0;\\n  for (const { topic, score } of topHits) {\\n    if (totalFacts >= RECALL_MAX_FACTS) break;\\n    const facts = readTopicFacts(userId, topic);\\n    if (facts.length === 0) continue;\\n    // Exclude facts already in the preamble tail\\n    const filtered = recentFacts\\n      ? facts.filter((f) => !recentFacts.has(f))\\n      : facts;\\n    if (filtered.length === 0) continue;\\n    const budget = RECALL_MAX_FACTS - totalFacts;\\n    const sliced = filtered.slice(-budget); // take most recent facts from shard\\n    totalFacts += sliced.length;\\n    results.push({ topic, score, facts: sliced });\\n  }\\n\\n  return results;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Format recall hits into a context block to prepend to the user message.\\n */\\nexport function formatRecallBlock(hits: RecallHit[]): string {\\n  if (hits.length === 0) return \\\"\\\";\\n  const sections = hits.map((h) => {\\n    const factLines = h.facts.map((f) => `- ${f}`).join(\\\"\\\\n\\\");\\n    return `[${h.topic}] (relevance ${h.score.toFixed(2)})\\\\n${factLines}`;\\n  });\\n  return (\\n    `[Memory recall — dynamically retrieved facts relevant to this message]\\\\n` +\\n    sections.join(\\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\") +\\n    `\\\\n[End memory recall]\\\\n\\\\n`\\n  );\\n}\\n\",\"numLines\":192,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":192}}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

process.env.MEMORY_DIR\\n    : \\\"F:\\\\\\\\AI\\\\\\\\AIMemory\\\"\\n);\\n\\nfunction activeUserId(): number {\\n  // Primary: TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID — set automatically by the telegram-kiro\\n  // bridge when it spawns the agent. Set one of:\\\\n\\\" +\\n      \\\"  - env TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID (how the telegram-kiro bridge normally injects it), or\\\\n\\\" +\\n      \\\"  - env MEMORY_USER_ID (set in ~/.codex/config.toml [mcp_servers.memory.env] for standalone Codex use), or\\\\n\\\" +\\n      `  - a plain-text file ${path.join(MEMORY_DIR, \\\".user-id\\\")} containing just the Telegram user id.`\\n  );\\n}\\n\\nfunction factsPath(userId: number): string {\\n  return path.join(MEMORY_DIR, `facts-${userId}.md`);\\n}\\n\\nasync function remember(text: string, topic?: string): Promise<string> {\\n  const line = text.trim();\\n  if (!line) throw new Error(\\\"text is empty\\\");\\n  const userId = activeUserId();\\n  await mkdir(MEMORY_DIR, { recursive: true });\\n\\n  // Dedup check: precise content hash (SQLite) then Jaccard fallback.\\n  const targetTopic = topic?.trim() || (await classifyFactAsync(line)) || \\\"misc\\\";\\n  if (factExists(line)) {\\n    return `⚠️ Exact duplicate detected (content hash match). Returns the most similar existing fact\\n * text if similarity > 0.7, or null if no duplicate found.\\n */\\nfunction checkDuplicate(newFact: string, existingFacts: string[]): string | null {\\n  if (existingFacts.length === 0) return null;\\n  const newWords = wordSet(newFact);\\n  if (newWords.size < 3) return null; // too short to meaningfully compare\\n  let bestScore = 0;\\n  let bestFact = \\\"\\\";\\n  for (const existing of existingFacts) {\\n    const existWords = wordSet(existing);\\n    const intersection = new Set([...newWords].filter((w) => existWords.has(w)));\\n    const union = new Set([...newWords, ...existWords]);\\n    const jaccard = union.size > 0 ? intersection.size / union.size : 0;\\n    if (jaccard > bestScore) {\\n      bestScore = jaccard;\\n      bestFact = existing;\\n    }\\n  }\\n  return bestScore >= 0.7 ? bestFact : null;\\n}\\n\\nfunction wordSet(text: string): Set<string> {\\n  return new Set(\\n    text.toLowerCase().replace(/[^\\\\w\\\\u4e00-\\\\u9fff]+/g, \\\" \\\").trim().split(/\\\\s+/).filter(Boolean)\\n  );\",\"numLines\":150,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":757}}"</outcome>

 // Tier 4: transient/catchall (slight penalty)\\n  \\\"misc\\\": 0.85,\\n};\\nconst DEFAULT_TIER_BOOST = 1.0;\\n\\n// ─── State path.join(resolveMemoryDir(), DB_FILENAME);\\n  db = new Database(dbPath);\\n  db.pragma(\\\"journal_mode = WAL\\\");\\n  db.pragma(\\\"synchronous = NORMAL\\\");\\n  createSchema(db);\\n}\\n\\nexport function closeMemoryDb(): void {\\n  db?.close();\\n  db = null;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Sync master log → SQLite. (!db) return { inserted: 0, deleted: 0 };\\n\\n  const masterPath = masterFactsFile(userId);\\n  const parsed = parseMasterLog(masterPath);\\n\\n  // Get existing hashes in DB\\n  const existingRows =  );\\n    const insFts = db.prepare(\\n      \\\"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO facts_fts (content_hash, text) VALUES (?, ?)\\\",\\n    );\\n    const tx = db.transaction(() => {\\n      for (const f of db.prepare(\\\"DELETE FROM facts_fts WHERE content_hash = ?\\\").run(hash);\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Check if a fact with the same content hash already exists.\\n */\\nexport function factExists(text: // topic name → vector\\nlet initialized = false;\\n\\n// ─── Public API ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/**\\n * Build topic vectors from current topics.json rules.\\n * Call after initEmbedRouter() so the model is loaded.\\n */\\nexport async function initMemoryRecall(): Promise<void> {\\n  if (!isEmbedRouterReady()) return;\\n  const rules = getTopicRules();\\n  topicVectors.clear();\\n  for (const rule of rules) {\\n    if (rule.topic === \\\"misc\\\") continue; // skip catchall\\n    // Build repr: \\\"topic-name: keyword1, keyword2, keyword3\\\"\\n    const repr = `${rule.topic}: ${rule.keywords.join(\\\", \\\")}`;\\n    const vec = await embedText(repr);\\n    if (vec) topicVectors.set(rule.topic, vec);\\n  }\\n  initialized = true;\\n  console.log(`[memory-recall] ready: ${topicVectors.size} topic vectors`);\\n}\\n\\nexport function isMemoryRecallReady(): boolean {\\n  return initialized && topicVectors.size > 0;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface RecallHit {\\n  topic: string;\\n  score: number;\\n  facts: string[]; t0}ms)`);\\n        if (reranked.length > 0) {\\n          rankedHits = reranked.map(r => ({\\n            text: r.text,\\n            topic: r.topic,\\n            score: r.rerankScore,\\n          })) as typeof hits;\\n        }\\n      } catch { /* rerank failed — use original order */ }\\n    }\\n\\n    // Group by topic for output format compatibility\\n    const byTopic = new Map<string, { score: number; facts: string[] }>();\\n    for (const h of rankedHits) {\\n      const topic = h.topic || \\\"misc\\\";\\n      if (excludeTopics?.has(topic)) continue;\\n      if (includeTopics && !includeTopics.has(topic)) continue;\\n      const entry = byTopic.get(topic) ?? { score: 0, facts: [] };\\n      entry.score = Math.max(entry.score, h.score);\\n      entry.facts.push(h.text);\\n      byTopic.set(topic, entry);\\n    }\\n\\n    const results: RecallHit[] = [];\\n    for (const [topic, { score, facts }] of byTopic) {\\n      results.push({ topic, score, facts });\\n    }\\n    results.sort((a, b) => b.score -   <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\scripts\\\\sync-skills-to-repo.mjs\",\"content\":\"#!/usr/bin/env node\\n/**\\n * sync-skills-to-repo.mjs — Copy tracked default-skills FROM local skill dir TO repo.\\n * Only syncs skills already in default-skills/ (won't add new ones).\\n * Called by .githooks/pre-commit automatically.\\n */\\nimport { readFileSync, writeFileSync, readdirSync, existsSync, mkdirSync } from \\\"node:fs\\\";\\nimport path from \\\"node:path\\\";\\nimport { fileURLToPath } from \\\"node:url\\\";\\nimport os from \\\"node:os\\\";\\n\\nconst __dirname = path.dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));\\nconst repoRoot = path.resolve(__dirname, \\\"..\\\");\\nconst defaultSkillsDir = path.join(repoRoot, \\\"default-skills\\\");\\nconst kiroSkillsDir = path.join(os.homedir(), \\\".kiro\\\", \\\"skills\\\");\\n\\nif (!existsSync(defaultSkillsDir)) process.exit(0);\\n\\nconst tracked = readdirSync(defaultSkillsDir, { withFileTypes: true })\\n  .filter(e => e.isDirectory())\\n  .map(e => e.name);\\n\\nlet updated = 0;\\nfor (const name of tracked) {\\n  const srcFile = path.join(kiroSkillsDir, name, \\\"SKILL.md\\\");\\n  const dstFile = path.join(defaultSkillsDir, name, \\\"SKILL.md\\\");\\n  if (!existsSync(srcFile)) continue;\\n  const src = readFileSync(srcFile, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n  const dst = existsSync(dstFile) ? readFileSync(dstFile, \\\"utf8\\\") : \\\"\\\";\\n  if (src === dst) continue;\\n  mkdirSync(path.dirname(dstFile), { recursive: true });\\n  writeFileSync(dstFile, src, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n  updated++;\\n}\\n\\nif (updated > 0) console.log(`[sync-skills] ${updated} skill(s) updated in default-skills/`);\\n\",\"numLines\":37,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":37}}"</outcome>

policies**，\\n而不是在這裡複製一份副本。\\n\\n## Project overview\\n\\n`telegram-kiro-bridge` 是一個 Telegram ↔ ACP agent 橋接器。Bridge 本身\\ncode 不綁特定 agent；目前支援 Kiro CLI、Codex（`@zed-industries/codex-acp`）、\\nClaude Agent（`claude-agent-acp`）三個 ACP adapter，透過 `.env` 切換。\\n\\n- 進入點：`src/index.ts`\\n- Session / preamble：`src/sessionManager.ts`、`src/memory.ts`\\n- Token protocol（`<<SEND_FILE>>` / `<<ASK>>` / `<<SCHEDULE>>` /\\n  `<<SKILL_USED>>` / `<<GOAL_DONE>>` / `<<RESTART>>` / `<<STICKER>>` /\\n  `<<WIKI_QUERY>>` / `<<SKILL_PROPOSE>>` / `<<SPECIALIST_PROXY>>` /\\n  `<<RELAY>>` / `<<RELAY_FILE>>` / `<<RELAY_DONE>>` /\\n  `<<RELAY_DELEGATE>>` / `<<PARALLEL_DELEGATE>>`）：\\n  `src/observerTransformer.ts` 是新 token 的統一抽取點；三個老 token\\n  （SEND_FILE / SCHEDULE / ASK）的 extract helper 仍在 `src/index.ts`\\n  （刻意不 refactor、零 regression）。\\n- Smoke scripts：`scripts/check-*.mjs`、`scripts/check-*.cmd`\\n\\n## Telegram bridge relay tools\\n\\n當需要直接回覆另一個 relay peer（例如小白 / 小X）時，使用這些 token；必須裸寫\\n在回覆中，不可包在 backtick 或 code block 內，否則 bridge 會把它當普通文字：\\n\\n- `<<RELAY:peer|message>>` — 傳純文字訊息給指定 peer。用於補狀態、確認收到、\\n  或繼續 relay 對話；不要把 token 寫成說明文字後期待它送出。\\n- `<<RELAY_FILE:peer|絕對路徑>>` — 傳檔給指定 peer。路徑必須在 bridge 允許的\\n  root 底下；圖片等檔案會走 Telegram relay file path。\\n- `<<RELAY_DONE>>` — 結束目前 relay 對話。只有在確認任務完成、無待辦事項時使用；\\n  單純收到對方檔案或告知使用者「圖片已送達」不代表要結束任務。\\n\\n如果這輪 preamble 沒列出 relay tool，但 repo `src/index.ts` 仍有 `<<RELAY:...>>`\\n解析邏輯，回覆 peer 時仍應使用實際支援的裸 token，而不是只向使用者描述。\\n\\n## Policies — must read before editing\\n\\n全部都在 [`POLICIES/`](./POLICIES/)。特別在動 preamble 或 Windows shell\\n相關 code 之前要讀：\\n\\n- [`POLICIES/preamble-frozen-snapshot.md`](./POLICIES/preamble-frozen-snapshot.md)\\n  — Preamble 每個 session 只 build 一次並凍結，中途禁止重建。改\\n  `buildPreamble*` 要過 change-this-policy checklist。\\n- [`POLICIES/windows-shell-safety.md`](./POLICIES/windows-shell-safety.md)\\n  — Windows 上讀檔 / 搜尋 / 列目錄走 dedicated tool，不要走\\n  `Get-Content` / `type` / `cat`。\\n- [`POLICIES/ai-md-convention.md`](./POLICIES/ai-md-convention.md)\\n  — 工作目錄放 `AI.md` 作為分層項目上下文；agent 讀到/改到某目錄時\\n  自動找 AI.md 讀取。≤2000 字、根寫架構子寫細節。\\n- [`POLICIES/proactive-write-policy.md`](./POLICIES/proactive-write-policy.md)\\n  — Agent 必須主動建立/更新 AI.md（≥3 檔案改動、架構變更、踩坑）和\\n  SPEC.md（≥3 工項跨多檔）。預設行為是寫，不是不寫。\\n\\n完整索引 + 新增 policy 的流程見 [`POLICIES/README.md`](./POLICIES/README.md)。\\n\\n## CodeGraph — projectPath 必須對準目標專案\\n\\ncodegraph 工具**預設讀 current working directory**（即本 repo\\n`F:/AI/telegram-kiro-bridge`）。當你在操作其他專案時，**不帶 projectPath\\n就會搜到 bridge 的 codebase 而非目標專案**——這是常見錯誤。\\n\\n規則：**每次呼叫 codegraph 前確認 projectPath 指向你正在改的專案**。\\n\\n```\\n# 操作 UK815 slot 時\\ncodegraph_context({ task: \\\"...\\\", projectPath: \\\"E:/UK/uk_815_pirates_queen_client\\\" })\\n\\n# 操作 bridge 本身時（可省略，因為 cwd 就是 bridge）\\ncodegraph_context({ task: \\\"...\\\" })\\n```\\n\\n常用操作：\\n- 改函式簽名前用 `codegraph_impact` 確認影響範圍\\n- 追蹤呼叫鏈用 `codegraph_trace`\\n- 初次接觸某功能用 `codegraph_context` 取得全景\\n\\n## Build / verify\\n\\n```bash\\nnpm install\\nnpx tsc --noEmit              # 改完 src/ 必跑\\nnode scripts/check-*.mjs      # smoke suite，相關 feature 改動要跑對應 script\\n```\\n\\n## 開發偏好\\n\\n- `strReplace` 改完**立刻 grep / read 驗證**，防 Kiro-style silent-fail。\\n- 大檔 refactor 用 ast-grep `pattern_rewrite` + `dry_run=true` 先看 pattern 覆蓋。\\n- Scope 可縮不可膨脹；refactor + 新功能合併 = 風險放大，盡量拆開 PR。\\n- Mid-session 絕不 rebuild preamble — 見 `POLICIES/preamble-frozen-snapshot.md`。\",\"numLines\":93,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":93}}"</outcome>

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <what_happened>Read</what_happened>

  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\AI.md\",\"content\":\"# telegram-kiro-bridge\\n\\n## 架構\\n\\nTelegram Bot ↔ Bridge (TypeScript/Node.js) ↔ ACP Agent (Kiro/Codex/Claude)。\\nBridge 負責 session 管理、preamble 注入、token 解析、enrichment pipeline、MCP memory。\\n主入口 `src/index.ts`（~1467 行），指令 handler 拆至 `src/commands/`，核心邏輯拆至獨立模組。\\n\\n## 模組分工\\n\\n- `index.ts` — Telegram bot 主迴圈 + per-turn enrichment pipeline + callback_query dispatcher + specialist proxy glue（~1467 行）\\n- `bot-setup.ts` — createBot factory（含 rate-limit observer）+ CommandSpec + COMMAND_SPECS + keyboard builders + registerBotCommands\\n- `run-prompt.ts` — runPrompt 核心（streaming edit、token dispatch、goal continuation、specialist proxy）\\n- `working-state.ts` — WorkingState 寫入 + delegation context 存取 + memory token helpers\\n- `session-extract.ts` — transcript 儲存、fact 萃取、handleRestart、pending-extract queue\\n- `commands/goal.ts` — /goal 狀態機指令\\n- `commands/dream.ts` — /dream 夜間維運宏\\n- `commands/schedule.ts` — /schedule 排程管理\\n- `commands/memory.ts` — /remember /memory /summary /memorytoskill + specialist-review\\n- `commands/misc.ts` — /context /usage /backup /save /wikisync /factlint 等雜項\\n- `commands/relay.ts` — relay bot handlers、relay poller init、specialist proxy、delegation context helpers\\n- `commands/deps.ts` — commands 共用依賴注入（避免循環 import）\\n- `memory.ts` — preamble 建構（buildPreambleWithBreakdown）+ fact CRUD\\n- `sessionManager.ts` — ACP session lifecycle（create/close/idle sweep）\\n- `acpClient.ts` — ACP protocol adapter（spawn agent、send prompt、stream response）\\n- `observerTransformer.ts` — 解析 agent 回覆中的 token（ASK/SEND_FILE/SCHEDULE/STICKER/...）\\n- `specialist.ts` + `specialist-create.ts` — 本機 specialist 分身（proxy/delegate/parallel）\\n-
