content.thinking:
Now I can see what the user is investigating. They're examining how the bridge system detects and integrates with different agent platforms (Kiro, Codex, Claude Code). The key findings:

1. The system detects which agent is running by checking the `config.agent?.command` string
2. It maps this to config directories:
   - Kiro → ~/.kiro
   - Codex → ~/.codex  
   - Claude → ~/.claude

3. The `detectAgentConfigDir()` and `detectSkillDir()` functions handle this detection

The user's original question was "目前 bridge 有跟 我的個人 AI 作業系統 好好配合嗎?" which translates to "Is the bridge currently working well with my personal AI operating system?"

They're now understanding how the bridge adapts to different agent platforms. This is discovery/exploration work. Let me wait to see if they make any actual changes or decisions.

from \\\"./commands/schedule.js\\\";\\nimport { estimateTokens, pct } from \\\"./commands/misc.js\\\";\\nimport { formatRelative } from \\\"./utils.js\\\";\\nimport { autoWikilink } from \\\"./wiki-retrieval.js\\\";\\nimport { parseRecurrence, computeNextRunAt, formatRecurrence } from \\\"./recurrence.js\\\";\\nimport { ScheduleStore } from \\\"./schedule-store.js\\\";\\nimport { Scheduler } from \\\"./scheduler.js\\\";\\nimport type { ContinuationContext } from \\\"./types.js\\\";\\n\\n// ─── Types ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/** Same shape as ContinuationContext; re-aliased so the runPrompt body\\n * can distinguish \\\"plan scheduled for next turn\\\" from \\\"we're currently\\n * running one\\\" at the type level. */\\ntype ContinuationPlan = ContinuationContext;\\n\\nexport type RunPromptMode =\\n  | { kind: \\\"user\\\" }\\n  | { kind: \\\"continuation\\\"; context: ContinuationContext }\\n  | { kind: \\\"meta\\\"; silent?: boolean }\\n  | { kind: \\\"relay\\\"; replyTo: string };\\n\\n// ─── Sticker auto-selection ──────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nconst EMOTION_REPRS: Record<string, string> = {\\n  hello: \\\"打招呼 你好 嗨 早安 晚安 greeting\\\",\\n  happy: \\\"開心 太好了 成功 完成了 恭喜 great\\\",\\n  thanks: \\\"謝謝 感謝 thank you 不客氣\\\",\\n  sorry: \\\"抱歉 對不起 不好意思 sorry\\\",\\n  agree: \\\"同意 沒問題 好的 了解 ok sure\\\",\\n  good: \\\"做得好 讚 很棒 nice well done\\\",\\n  clap: \\\"恭喜 太厲害了 完美 bravo\\\",\\n  love: \\\"喜歡 愛 太棒了 超讚\\\",\\n  sad: \\\"可惜 遺憾 不幸 unfortunately\\\",\\n  shock: \\\"驚訝 天啊 沒想到 wow unexpected\\\",\\n  ok: \\\"收到 了解 知道了 acknowledged\\\",\\n};\\nconst STICKER_AUTO_THRESHOLD = 0.65;\\nconst STICKER_AUTO_MAX_REPLY_LEN = 150;\\nlet emotionVectors: Map<string, Float32Array> | null = null;\\n\\nexport async function autoSelectSticker(reply: string): Promise<string | null> {\\n  if (reply.length > STICKER_AUTO_MAX_REPLY_LEN || reply.length < 5) return null;\\n  if (!emotionVectors) {\\n    emotionVectors = new Map();\\n    for (const [emotion, repr] of Object.entries(EMOTION_REPRS)) {\\n      const vec = await embedText(repr);\\n      if (vec) emotionVectors.set(emotion, vec);\\n    }\\n  }\\n  const queryVec = await embedText(reply);\\n  if (!queryVec) return null;\\n  let best = \\\"\\\";\\n  let bestScore = 0;\\n  for (const [emotion, vec] of emotionVectors) {\\n    const score = cosineSim(queryVec, vec);\\n    if (score > bestScore) { bestScore = score; best = emotion; }\\n  }\\n  return bestScore >= STICKER_AUTO_THRESHOLD ? best : null;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Continuation timer ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nconst GOAL_CONTINUATION_DELAY_MS = 500;\\nconst pendingContinuations = new Map<number, NodeJS.Timeout>();\\n\\nexport function cancelPendingContinuation(chatId: number): void {\\n  const h = pendingContinuations.get(chatId);\\n  if (h) {\\n    clearTimeout(h);\\n    pendingContinuations.delete(chatId);\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Deps interface — mutable state that lives in index.ts ───────────────────\\n\\nexport interface RunPromptDeps {\\n  bot: Bot;\\n  sessions: SessionManager;\\n  rateLimitedUntil: () => number;\\n  onRateLimitRecovery: (fn: () => void | Promise<void>) => () => void;\\n  queuePendingMessage: (chatId: number, text: string) => void;\\n  handleRestart: (ctx: Context, opts?: { reason?: string }) => Promise<void>;\\n  STREAM_EDIT_INTERVAL_MS: number;\\n  MEMORY_DIR: string;\\n  scheduler: Scheduler;\\n  scheduleStore: ScheduleStore;\\n}\\n\\nlet _deps: RunPromptDeps | null = null;\\n\\nexport function initRunPrompt(deps: RunPromptDeps): void {\\n  _deps = deps;\\n}\\n\\nfunction deps(): RunPromptDeps {\\n  if (!_deps) throw new Error(\\\"runPrompt not initialized — call initRunPrompt first\\\");\\n  return _deps;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Auto-wikilink post-processing ───────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/** After a wiki page is written, scan it and inject [[wikilinks]] for known entities. */\\nfunction applyAutoWikilink(slug: string): void {\\n  const { MEMORY_DIR } = deps();\\n  for (const sub of [\\\"queries\\\", \\\"concepts\\\", \\\"references\\\", \\\"lessons\\\", \\\"tasks\\\"]) {\\n    const filePath = path.join(MEMORY_DIR, \\\"wiki\\\", sub, `${slug}.md`);\\n    if (existsSync(filePath)) {\\n      try {\\n        const content = readFileSync(filePath, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n        const linked = autoWikilink(content);\\n        if (linked !== content) {\\n          writeFileSync(filePath, linked, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n          console.log(`[auto-wikilink] injected links into wiki/${sub}/${slug}.md`);\\n        }\\n      } catch (err) {\\n        console.error(`[auto-wikilink] failed for ${slug}:`, err);\\n      }\\n      return;\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ─── runPrompt ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nexport async function runPrompt(\\n  ctx: Context,\\n  session: ChatSession,\\n  text: string,\\n  mode: RunPromptMode = { kind: \\\"user\\\" },\\n) {\\n  const { bot, sessions, rateLimitedUntil, onRateLimitRecovery, queuePendingMessage,\\n    handleRestart, mode.replyTo : undefined;\\n  const isRelay = mode.kind === \\\"relay\\\";\\n  const chatId = ctx.chat!.id;\\n\\n  // Guard against concurrent prompts on the same ACP session.\\n  if (session.inflight) {\\n    if (relayReplyTo) {\\n      sendRelay(relayReplyTo, \\\"小X 目前上一輪還在處理中，稍後再回覆。\\\").catch((err) =>\\n        console.error(`[chat ${chatId}] relay busy notice to ${relayReplyTo} failed:`, err),\\n      );\\n      notifyRelayOwner(\\n        bot,\\n        config.allowedUserIds[0] ?? 0,\\n        `⏳ 「${relayReplyTo}」又送來 relay 訊息，但上一輪還在處理中，已擋掉這次重複注入。`,\\n        `busy notice for ${relayReplyTo}`,\\n      );\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    if (isMeta) {\\n      console.log(`[chat ${chatId}] meta prompt queued (session inflight)`);\\n      session._pendingMetaQueue.push({ ctx: ctx as unknown as any, prompt: text, opts: mode as any });\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    await ctx.reply(\\n      \\\"⏳ 上一個回合還在進行中。請等它結束，或先用 /cancel 取消。\\\",\\n    );\\n    return;\\n  }\\n  session.inflight = true;\",\"numLines\":200,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":987}}"</outcome>

content.thinking:
The user is examining how the telegram-kiro-bridge integrates with their "個人 AI 作業系統" (personal AI operating system). They're specifically investigating:

1. Skill directory detection and cross-agent compatibility (Kiro/Codex/Claude Code)
2. How skills are loaded and injected into the preamble
3. The enrichment pipeline (memory recall, wiki retrieval, skill routing)
4. How the bridge detects and adapts to different AI agents

Key findings so far:
- The bridge uses `detectAgentConfigDir()` to auto-detect which agent platform is running (Kiro/Codex/Claude Code) based on the command name
- Skills are stored in `~/.kiro/skills`, `~/.codex/skills`, or `~/.claude/skills` depending on the platform
- The `skill-router.ts` module uses semantic embedding matching to suggest relevant skills based on user messages
- Skills are integrated into the preamble via the enrichment pipeline
- There's a sync mechanism (`sync-skills-to-repo.mjs`) to keep default skills in sync

The user's original question was "目前 bridge 有跟 我的個人 AI 作業系統 好好配合嗎?" - "Is the bridge currently working well with my personal AI operating system?"

They're conducting a thorough investigation of the integration points. This is still in the discovery phase - no code changes or decisions have been made yet.

  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\src\\\\provider\\\\mcp-pool.ts\",\"content\":\"/**\\n * MCP Client Pool — connects to configured MCP servers (stdio transport),\\n * aggregates their tool schemas, and routes tool_use calls to the right server.\\n *\\n * Used by DirectProvider's tool loop. ACP mode doesn't need this (Kiro CLI\\n * manages its own MCP connections).\\n */\\nimport { Client } from \\\"@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/index.js\\\";\\nimport { StdioClientTransport } from \\\"@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/stdio.js\\\";\\nimport { readFile } from \\\"node:fs/promises\\\";\\nimport path from \\\"node:path\\\";\\n\\nexport interface McpServerConfig {\\n  command: string;\\n  args?: string[];\\n  env?: Record<string, string>;\\n  disabled?: boolean;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface ToolSchema {\\n  name: string;\\n  description?: string;\\n  inputSchema: Record<string, unknown>;\\n  /** Which MCP server owns this tool. */\\n  _server: string;\\n}\\n\\ninterface ConnectedServer {\\n  name: string;\\n  client: Client;\\n  transport: StdioClientTransport;\\n  tools: ToolSchema[];\\n}\\n\\nexport class McpPool {\\n  private servers: ConnectedServer[] = [];\\n  private toolIndex = new Map<string, ConnectedServer>();\\n\\n  /** Connect to all enabled MCP servers from a config object. */\\n  async connectAll(configs: Record<string, McpServerConfig>, extraEnv?: Record<string, string>): Promise<void> {\\n    const entries = Object.entries(configs).filter(([, c]) => !c.disabled);\\n    const results = await Promise.allSettled(\\n      entries.map(([name, cfg]) => this.connectOne(name, cfg, extraEnv)),\\n    );\\n    for (let i = 0; i < results.length; i++) {\\n      if (results[i].status === \\\"rejected\\\") {\\n        console.warn(`[mcp-pool] failed to connect \\\"${entries[i][0]}\\\":`, (results[i] as PromiseRejectedResult).reason?.message ?? results[i]);\\n      }\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  private async connectOne(name: string, cfg: McpServerConfig, extraEnv?: Record<string, string>): Promise<void> {\\n    const transport = new StdioClientTransport({\\n      command: cfg.command,\\n      args: cfg.args ?? [],\\n      env: { ...process.env, ...cfg.env, ...extraEnv } as Record<string, string>,\\n    });\\n    const client = new Client({ name: `bridge-pool-${name}`, version: \\\"1.0.0\\\" });\\n    await client.connect(transport);\\n\\n    const { tools } = await client.listTools();\\n    const schemas: ToolSchema[] = (tools ?? []).map((t) => ({\\n      name: t.name,\\n      description: t.description,\\n      inputSchema: t.inputSchema as Record<string, unknown>,\\n      _server: name,\\n    }));\\n\\n    const server: ConnectedServer = { name, client, transport, tools: schemas };\\n    this.servers.push(server);\\n    for (const t of schemas) {\\n      this.toolIndex.set(t.name, server);\\n    }\\n    console.log(`[mcp-pool] connected \\\"${name}\\\" — ${schemas.length} tools`);\\n  }\\n\\n  /** All tool schemas across all connected servers (for LLM API tools param). */\\n  listTools(): ToolSchema[] {\\n    return this.servers.flatMap((s) => s.tools);\\n  }\\n\\n  /** Execute a tool call, routing to the correct MCP server. */\\n  async callTool(name: string, args: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<{ content: unknown[]; isError?: boolean }> {\\n    const server = this.toolIndex.get(name);\\n    if (!server) {\\n      return { content: [{ type: \\\"text\\\", text: `Error: tool \\\"${name}\\\" not found in any MCP server` }], isError: true };\\n    }\\n    const result = await server.client.callTool({ name, arguments: args });\\n    return { content: result.content as unknown[], isError: !!result.isError };\\n  }\\n\\n  /** Gracefully close all connections. */\\n  async close(): Promise<void> {\\n    await Promise.allSettled(this.servers.map((s) => s.client.close()));\\n    this.servers = [];\\n    this.toolIndex.clear();\\n  }\\n\\n  get size(): number {\\n    return this.servers.length;\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Load MCP server configs from ~/.kiro/settings/mcp.json.\\n * Also includes the bridge's own memory MCP server.\\n */\\nexport async function loadMcpConfigs(): Promise<Record<string, McpServerConfig>> {\\n  const settingsPath = path.join(\\n    process.env.AGENT_CONFIG_DIR ?? path.join(process.env.USERPROFILE ?? process.env.HOME ?? \\\"\\\", \\\".kiro\\\"),\\n    \\\"settings\\\",\\n    \\\"mcp.json\\\",\\n  );\\n  try {\\n    const raw = await readFile(settingsPath, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n    const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);\\n    const configs: Record<string, McpServerConfig> = parsed.mcpServers ?? {};\\n    // Always include the bridge's own memory MCP server\\n    if (!configs[\\\"memory\\\"]) {\\n      configs[\\\"memory\\\"] = {\\n        command: \\\"node\\\",\\n        args: [path.resolve(\\\"dist/mcp-memory.js\\\")],\\n      };\\n    }\\n    return configs;\\n  } catch (err) {\\n    console.warn(`[mcp-pool] could not load ${settingsPath}:`, (err as Error).message);\\n    // Fallback: just the memory server\\n    return {\\n      memory: { command: \\\"node\\\", args: [path.resolve(\\\"dist/mcp-memory.js\\\")] },\\n    };\\n  }\\n}\\n\",\"numLines\":134,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":134}}"</outcome>

aiTitle: 評估 bridge 與個人 AI 作業系統的整合狀態

content.thinking:
The user has been conducting a thorough investigation of the telegram-kiro-bridge architecture, specifically focusing on:

1. **Cross-agent platform compatibility**: Examining how the bridge detects and integrates with different AI agent platforms (Kiro/Codex/Claude Code) via `detectAgentConfigDir()` and `detectSkillDir()`
2. **Skill integration**: Understanding how skills from `~/.kiro/skills`, `~/.codex/skills`, or `~/.claude/skills` are loaded and routed semantically
3. **Preamble construction**: How the bridge builds context for agents with memory, skills, environment variables
4. **Enrichment pipeline**: The multi-stage per-turn injection system (NotebookLM routing, wiki retrieval, memory recall, skill routing)

The user initially asked: "目前 bridge 有跟 我的個人 AI 作業系統 好好配合嗎?" (Is the bridge working well with my personal AI operating system?)

They've spent considerable time reading:
- Architecture documentation (AI.md, CLAUDE.md)
- Core modules (sessionManager.ts, memory.ts, skill-router.ts, enrichment.ts, run-prompt.ts, index.ts)
- Platform detection logic (detectAgentConfigDir, detectSkillDir)
- Backup integration (backup.ts showing steering/skills/agents backup)
- Skill sync mechanism (sync-skills-to-repo.mjs)

The investigation appears to be understanding **how the bridge auto-detects and integrates with different agent platforms** (Kiro/Codex/Claude Code), particularly around:
- Skill directories (~/.kiro/skills vs ~/.codex/skills vs ~/.claude/skills)
- Agent config directories
- Preamble injection of environment variables (SKILL_DIR, AGENT_CONFIG_DIR)
- Backup of steering/skills/agents directories

This is discovery/investigation work, not implementation yet. 

No code changes have been made yet. The user is still in the reading phase, building understanding of how the bridge's integration layer connects with the broader personal AI operating system. I should wait for them to either make code modifications, articulate specific findings, or ask clarifying questions before documenting conclusions.

[]).map((t) => ({\\n      name: t.name,\\n      description: t.description,\\n      inputSchema: t.inputSchema as Record<string, unknown>,\\n      _server: name,\\n    }));\\n\\n    const server: ConnectedServer = { name, client, transport, tools: schemas };\\n    this.servers.push(server);\\n    for (const t of schemas) {\\n      this.toolIndex.set(t.name, server);\\n    }\\n    console.log(`[mcp-pool] connected \\\"${name}\\\" — ${schemas.length} tools`);\\n  }\\n\\n  /** All tool schemas across all connected servers (for LLM API tools param). */\\n  listTools(): ToolSchema[] {\\n    return this.servers.flatMap((s) => s.tools);\\n  }\\n\\n  /** Execute a tool call, routing to the correct MCP server. */\\n  async callTool(name: string, args: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<{ content: unknown[]; isError?: boolean }> {\\n    const server = this.toolIndex.get(name);\\n    if (!server) {\\n      return { content: [{ type: \\\"text\\\", text: `Error: tool \\\"${name}\\\" not found in any MCP server` }], isError: true };\\n    }\\n    const result = await server.client.callTool({ name, arguments: args });\\n    return { content: result.content as unknown[], isError: !!result.isError };\\n  }\\n\\n  /** Gracefully close all connections. */\\n  async close(): Promise<void> {\\n    await Promise.allSettled(this.servers.map((s) => s.client.close()));\\n    this.servers = [];\\n    this.toolIndex.clear();\\n  }\\n\\n  get size(): number {\\n    return this.servers.length;\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Load MCP server configs from ~/.kiro/settings/mcp.json.\\n * Also includes the bridge's own memory MCP server.\\n */\\nexport async function loadMcpConfigs(): Promise<Record<string, McpServerConfig>> {\\n  const settingsPath = path.join(\\n    process.env.AGENT_CONFIG_DIR ?? path.join(process.env.USERPROFILE ?? process.env.HOME ?? \\\"\\\", \\\".kiro\\\"),\\n    \\\"settings\\\",\\n    \\\"mcp.json\\\",\\n  );\\n  try {\\n    const raw = await readFile(settingsPath, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n    const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);\\n    const configs: Record<string, McpServerConfig> = parsed.mcpServers ?? {};\\n    // Always include the bridge's own memory MCP server\\n    if (!configs[\\\"memory\\\"]) {\\n      configs[\\\"memory\\\"] = {\\n        command: \\\"node\\\",\\n        args: [path.resolve(\\\"dist/mcp-memory.js\\\")],\\n      };\\n    }\\n    return configs;\\n  } catch (err) {\\n    console.warn(`[mcp-pool] could not load ${settingsPath}:`, (err as Error).message);\\n    // Fallback: just the memory server\\n    return {\\n      memory: { command: \\\"node\\\", args: [path.resolve(\\\"dist/mcp-memory.js\\\")] },\\n    };\\n  }\\n}\\n\",\"numLines\":134,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":134}}"</outcome>

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Bridge multi-agent platform compatibility verification</title>
  <subtitle>Investigated cross-platform agent detection and skill directory resolution for Kiro/Codex/Claude Code integration</subtitle>

    <fact>Backup system mirrors platform-specific directories (agents/settings/steering/powers/skills) to destination matching detected agent (kiro/, codex/, claude/)</fact>
    <fact>Enrichment pipeline integrates skill-router (semantic skill matching), memory-recall (topic-based retrieval), wiki-retrieval, and intent classification</fact>
    <fact>MCP pool loads server configs from ${AGENT_CONFIG_DIR}/settings/mcp.json with platform-aware fallback to .kiro if unset</fact>
    <fact>Platform detection fallback chain: env override → command name heuristic → filesystem probe (~/.kiro then ~/.codex)</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>User conducted comprehensive architectural review to verify bridge integration with personal AI operating system spanning multiple agent platforms (Kiro CLI, Codex, Claude Code). Investigation focused on cross-platform compatibility mechanisms:

**Platform Detection**: Bridge resolves agent home directory via detectAgentConfigDir() by parsing config.agent.command for platform hints ("kiro"|"codex"|"claude"), with env var AGENT_CONFIG_DIR as override. SKILL_DIR derives as ${AGENT_CONFIG_DIR}/skills unless explicitly overridden.

**Preamble Environment Block**: buildEnvironmentBlock() injects ${MEMORY_DIR}, ${SKILL_DIR}, ${AGENT_CONFIG_DIR}, ${USER_ID} into preamble so skills and memory tokens reference platform-agnostic variables instead of hardcoded machine-specific paths. This allows same SKILL.md to work across Kiro box, Codex box, and Claude Code.

**Backup Strategy**: /backup command mirrors AIMemory + agent config trees to GitHub repo with platform-aware subdirectories (kiro/, codex/, claude/) based on detected agent, enabling multi-machine backup repo layout.

**Skill Integration**: initSkillRouter() builds semantic vectors from SKILL.md frontmatter descriptions at ${SKILL_DIR}, enabling per-turn skill routing hints via embedding similarity. Skills are session-deduplicated (injectedSkills Set) to avoid repetition.

**Enrichment Pipeline**: Per-turn enrichment runs NotebookLM routing, wiki-retrieval, memory-recall, index-retrieval, and skill-router in sequence, all platform-agnostic via env-based path resolution.

No code changes made—pure discovery to verify existing multi-agent ecosystem integration correctness.</narrative>

Points\\n1. **Memory is the architectural centerpiece**: facts → preamble + dynamic recall + MCP tools + SQLite index creates a robust multi-tier system.\\n2. **Semantic routing works well**: skill and wiki retrieval use embeddings with per-session dedup to avoid redundant hints.\\n3. **Portability via env vars**: `${MEMORY_DIR}`, `${SKILL_DIR}`, `${AGENT_CONFIG_DIR}`, `${USER_ID}` allow the <observed_from_primary_session>
  <what_happened>Read</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-06-22T09:28:31.778Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>G:\AI\telegram-kiro-bridge-main</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"file_path\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\scripts\\\\sync-skills-to-repo.mjs\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\scripts\\\\sync-skills-to-repo.mjs\",\"content\":\"#!/usr/bin/env node\\n/**\\n * sync-skills-to-repo.mjs — Copy tracked default-skills FROM local skill dir TO repo.\\n * Only syncs skills already in default-skills/ (won't add new ones).\\n * Called by .githooks/pre-commit automatically.\\n */\\nimport { readFileSync, writeFileSync, readdirSync, existsSync, mkdirSync } from \\\"node:fs\\\";\\nimport path from \\\"node:path\\\";\\nimport { fileURLToPath } from \\\"node:url\\\";\\nimport os from \\\"node:os\\\";\\n\\nconst __dirname = path.dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));\\nconst repoRoot = path.resolve(__dirname, \\\"..\\\");\\nconst defaultSkillsDir = path.join(repoRoot, \\\"default-skills\\\");\\nconst kiroSkillsDir = path.join(os.homedir(), \\\".kiro\\\", \\\"skills\\\");\\n\\nif (!existsSync(defaultSkillsDir)) process.exit(0);\\n\\nconst tracked = readdirSync(defaultSkillsDir, { withFileTypes: true })\\n  .filter(e => e.isDirectory())\\n  .map(e => e.name);\\n\\nlet updated = 0;\\nfor (const name of tracked) {\\n  const srcFile = path.join(kiroSkillsDir, name, \\\"SKILL.md\\\");\\n  const dstFile = path.join(defaultSkillsDir, name, \\\"SKILL.md\\\");\\n  if (!existsSync(srcFile)) continue;\\n  const src = readFileSync(srcFile, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n  const dst = existsSync(dstFile) ? readFileSync(dstFile, \\\"utf8\\\") : \\\"\\\";\\n  if (src === dst) continue;\\n  mkdirSync(path.dirname(dstFile), { recursive: true });\\n  writeFileSync(dstFile, src, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n  updated++;\\n}\\n\\nif (updated > 0) console.log(`[sync-skills] ${updated} skill(s) updated in default-skills/`);\\n\",\"numLines\":37,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":37}}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>
